search_text: Active Perception Applied To Unmanned Aerial Vehicles Through Deep Reinforcement Learning 通过深度强化学习将主动感知应用于无人机 Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAV) have been standing out due to the wide range of applications in which they can be used autonomously. However, they need intelligent systems capable of providing a greater understanding of what they perceive to perform several tasks. They become more challenging in complex environments since there is a need to perceive the environment and act under environmental uncertainties to make a decision. In this context, a system that uses active perception can improve performance by seeking the best next view through the recognition of targets while displacement occurs. This work aims to contribute to the active perception of UAVs by tackling the problem of tracking and recognizing water surface structures to perform a dynamic landing. We show that our system with classical image processing techniques and a simple Deep Reinforcement Learning (Deep-RL) agent is capable of perceiving the environment and dealing with uncertainties without making the use of complex Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) or Contrastive Learning (CL). 无人机（UAV）因其可以自主使用的广泛应用而脱颖而出。然而，他们需要能够更好地理解他们执行多项任务的感知的智能系统。在复杂的环境中它们变得更具挑战性，因为需要感知环境并在环境不确定性下采取行动以做出决策。在这种情况下，使用主动感知的系统可以通过在发生位移时识别目标来寻求最佳的下一个视图，从而提高性能。这项工作旨在通过解决跟踪和识别水面结构以执行动态着陆的问题，为无人机的主动感知做出贡献。我们证明，我们的系统采用经典图像处理技术和简单的深度强化学习（Deep-RL）代理，能够感知环境并处理不确定性，而无需使用复杂的卷积神经网络（CNN）或对比学习（CL）。 本文将主动感知应用于无人机，通过深度强化学习解决动态着陆中的水面结构跟踪与识别问题。 Matheus G. Mateus,Ricardo B. Grando,Paulo L. J. Drews-Jr cs.RO,cs.AI

authors_text: Ricardo B. Grando,Junior C. de Jesus,Victor A. Kich,Alisson H. Kolling,Rodrigo S. Guerra,Paulo L. J. Drews-Jr
title: Mapless Navigation of a Hybrid Aerial Underwater Vehicle with Deep Reinforcement Learning Through Environmental Generalization
title_zh: 通过环境泛化进行深度强化学习的混合空中水下航行器的无地图导航
comment: Paper accepted to the Latin American Robotics Symposium 2022
comments: Paper accepted to the Latin American Robotics Symposium 2022
abstract: Previous works showed that Deep-RL can be applied to perform mapless navigation, including the medium transition of Hybrid Unmanned Aerial Underwater Vehicles (HUAUVs). This paper presents new approaches based on the state-of-the-art actor-critic algorithms to address the navigation and medium transition problems for a HUAUV. We show that a double critic Deep-RL with Recurrent Neural Networks improves the navigation performance of HUAUVs using solely range data and relative localization. Our Deep-RL approaches achieved better navigation and transitioning capabilities with a solid generalization of learning through distinct simulated scenarios, outperforming previous approaches.
summary: Previous works showed that Deep-RL can be applied to perform mapless navigation, including the medium transition of Hybrid Unmanned Aerial Underwater Vehicles (HUAUVs). This paper presents new approaches based on the state-of-the-art actor-critic algorithms to address the navigation and medium transition problems for a HUAUV. We show that a double critic Deep-RL with Recurrent Neural Networks improves the navigation performance of HUAUVs using solely range data and relative localization. Our Deep-RL approaches achieved better navigation and transitioning capabilities with a solid generalization of learning through distinct simulated scenarios, outperforming previous approaches.
abstract_zh: 之前的工作表明，Deep-RL 可用于执行无地图导航，包括混合无人机水下航行器（HUAUV）的介质转换。本文提出了基于最先进的 actor-critic 算法的新方法，用于解决 HUAUV 的导航和介质转换问题。我们证明，具有循环神经网络的双重批评深度强化学习仅使用距离数据和相对定位来提高 HUAUV 的导航性能。我们的深度强化学习方法通​​过不同的模拟场景对学习进行了扎实的泛化，从而实现了更好的导航和转换能力，优于以前的方法。

AI.tldr: 本文提出基于深度强化学习的无地图导航方法，解决混合空中水下无人车的导航与介质转换问题。
AI.motivation: 现有无地图导航方法在环境泛化和复杂介质转换场景中存在性能不足，需提升适应性与鲁棒性。
AI.method: 采用双重评论深度强化学习框架结合循环神经网络，仅依赖距离数据和相对定位实现导航。
AI.result: 在多种模拟场景中导航与转换性能优于传统方法，验证了算法的环境泛化能力。
AI.conclusion: 深度强化学习有效提升了HUAUV的自主导航能力，为复杂环境下的多介质任务提供了新思路。

authors_text: Junchi Liang,Abdeslam Boularias
title: Learning Category-Level Manipulation Tasks from Point Clouds with Dynamic Graph CNNs
title_zh: 使用动态图 CNN 从点云学习类别级操作任务
abstract: This paper presents a new technique for learning category-level manipulation from raw RGB-D videos of task demonstrations, with no manual labels or annotations. Category-level learning aims to acquire skills that can be generalized to new objects, with geometries and textures that are different from the ones of the objects used in the demonstrations. We address this problem by first viewing both grasping and manipulation as special cases of tool use, where a tool object is moved to a sequence of key-poses defined in a frame of reference of a target object. Tool and target objects, along with their key-poses, are predicted using a dynamic graph convolutional neural network that takes as input an automatically segmented depth and color image of the entire scene. Empirical results on object manipulation tasks with a real robotic arm show that the proposed network can efficiently learn from real visual demonstrations to perform the tasks on novel objects within the same category, and outperforms alternative approaches.
summary: This paper presents a new technique for learning category-level manipulation from raw RGB-D videos of task demonstrations, with no manual labels or annotations. Category-level learning aims to acquire skills that can be generalized to new objects, with geometries and textures that are different from the ones of the objects used in the demonstrations. We address this problem by first viewing both grasping and manipulation as special cases of tool use, where a tool object is moved to a sequence of key-poses defined in a frame of reference of a target object. Tool and target objects, along with their key-poses, are predicted using a dynamic graph convolutional neural network that takes as input an automatically segmented depth and color image of the entire scene. Empirical results on object manipulation tasks with a real robotic arm show that the proposed network can efficiently learn from real visual demonstrations to perform the tasks on novel objects within the same category, and outperforms alternative approaches.
abstract_zh: 本文提出了一种从任务演示的原始 RGB-D 视频中学习类别级操作的新技术，无需手动标签或注释。类别级学习旨在获得可推广到新对象的技能，这些新对象的几何形状和纹理与演示中使用的对象不同。我们通过首先将抓取和操作视为工具使用的特殊情况来解决这个问题，其中工具对象被移动到目标对象的参考系中定义的一系列关键姿势。使用动态图卷积神经网络来预测工具和目标对象及其关键姿势，该网络将整个场景的自动分段深度和彩色图像作为输入。使用真实机械臂进行对象操作任务的经验结果表明，所提出的网络可以有效地从真实的视觉演示中学习，以在同一类别内的新对象上执行任务，并且优于替代方法。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种从RGB-D视频中学习类别级操作任务的新技术，无需人工标注，通过动态图卷积网络实现对新物体的泛化操作。
AI.motivation: 传统方法依赖人工标注数据，难以适应新物体的几何和纹理变化，需开发无需标注且能泛化的操作学习方法。
AI.method: 将抓取与操作视为工具使用，利用动态图卷积网络处理自动分割的深度和颜色图像，预测工具、目标物体及关键姿态。
AI.result: 实验证明该网络能通过真实视觉演示高效学习，在相同类别新物体上完成任务，优于现有方法。
AI.conclusion: 动态图卷积网络有效实现了从视觉演示中学习类别级操作，为机器人泛化操作提供了新思路。

authors_text: Ricardo B. Grando,Junior C. de Jesus,Victor A. Kich,Alisson H. Kolling,Rodrigo S. Guerra,Paulo L. J. Drews-Jr
title: Deterministic and Stochastic Analysis of Deep Reinforcement Learning for Low Dimensional Sensing-based Navigation of Mobile Robots
title_zh: 移动机器人低维感知导航深度强化学习的确定性和随机分析
comment: Paper accepted at the Latin American Robotics Symposium 2022
comments: Paper accepted at the Latin American Robotics Symposium 2022
abstract: Deterministic and Stochastic techniques in Deep Reinforcement Learning (Deep-RL) have become a promising solution to improve motion control and the decision-making tasks for a wide variety of robots. Previous works showed that these Deep-RL algorithms can be applied to perform mapless navigation of mobile robots in general. However, they tend to use simple sensing strategies since it has been shown that they perform poorly with a high dimensional state spaces, such as the ones yielded from image-based sensing. This paper presents a comparative analysis of two Deep-RL techniques - Deep Deterministic Policy Gradients (DDPG) and Soft Actor-Critic (SAC) - when performing tasks of mapless navigation for mobile robots. We aim to contribute by showing how the neural network architecture influences the learning itself, presenting quantitative results based on the time and distance of navigation of aerial mobile robots for each approach. Overall, our analysis of six distinct architectures highlights that the stochastic approach (SAC) better suits with deeper architectures, while the opposite happens with the deterministic approach (DDPG).
summary: Deterministic and Stochastic techniques in Deep Reinforcement Learning (Deep-RL) have become a promising solution to improve motion control and the decision-making tasks for a wide variety of robots. Previous works showed that these Deep-RL algorithms can be applied to perform mapless navigation of mobile robots in general. However, they tend to use simple sensing strategies since it has been shown that they perform poorly with a high dimensional state spaces, such as the ones yielded from image-based sensing. This paper presents a comparative analysis of two Deep-RL techniques - Deep Deterministic Policy Gradients (DDPG) and Soft Actor-Critic (SAC) - when performing tasks of mapless navigation for mobile robots. We aim to contribute by showing how the neural network architecture influences the learning itself, presenting quantitative results based on the time and distance of navigation of aerial mobile robots for each approach. Overall, our analysis of six distinct architectures highlights that the stochastic approach (SAC) better suits with deeper architectures, while the opposite happens with the deterministic approach (DDPG).
abstract_zh: 深度强化学习 (Deep-RL) 中的确定性和随机技术已成为改善各种机器人运动控制和决策任务的有前途的解决方案。之前的工作表明，这些 Deep-RL 算法通常可以应用于移动机器人的无地图导航。然而，它们倾向于使用简单的传感策略，因为已经证明它们在高维状态空间（例如基于图像的传感产生的状态空间）中表现不佳。本文对两种深度强化学习技术——深度确定性策略梯度（DDPG）和软执行者批评家（SAC）——在执行移动机器人无地图导航任务时进行了比较分析。我们的目标是展示神经网络架构如何影响学习本身，并根据每种方法的空中移动机器人的导航时间和距离提供定量结果。总体而言，我们对六种不同架构的分析强调，随机方法 (SAC) 更适合更深层次的架构，而确定性方法 (DDPG) 则相反。

AI.tldr: 本文比较了深度确定性策略梯度（DDPG）和软演员评论家（SAC）两种深度强化学习算法在低维传感导航任务中的性能差异。
AI.motivation: 现有研究因高维状态空间（如图像传感）导致性能下降，通常采用简单传感策略，但缺乏对不同神经网络架构与算法适配性的系统分析。
AI.method: 通过六种不同神经网络架构，定量评估DDPG和SAC在无人机导航任务中的时间与距离表现，对比其学习效率与稳定性。
AI.result: 实验表明，SAC算法在深层架构中表现更优，而DDPG在浅层架构中更具优势。
AI.conclusion: 算法选择应与网络深度匹配：深层架构优先采用SAC，浅层架构适用DDPG，为移动机器人导航设计提供架构选型依据。

authors_text: Samarth Kalluraya,George J. Pappas,Yiannis Kantaros
title: Multi-robot Mission Planning in Dynamic Semantic Environments
title_zh: 动态语义环境中的多机器人任务规划
comment: arXiv admin note: text overlap with arXiv:2012.10490
comments: arXiv admin note: text overlap with arXiv:2012.10490
abstract: This paper addresses a new semantic multi-robot planning problem in uncertain and dynamic environments. Particularly, the environment is occupied with non-cooperative, mobile, uncertain labeled targets. These targets are governed by stochastic dynamics while their current and future positions as well as their semantic labels are uncertain. Our goal is to control mobile sensing robots so that they can accomplish collaborative semantic tasks defined over the uncertain current/future positions and labels of these targets. We express these tasks using Linear Temporal Logic (LTL). We propose a sampling-based approach that explores the robot motion space, the mission specification space, as well as the future configurations of the labeled targets to design optimal paths. These paths are revised online to adapt to uncertain perceptual feedback. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first work that addresses semantic mission planning problems in uncertain and dynamic semantic environments. We provide extensive experiments that demonstrate the efficiency of the proposed method
summary: This paper addresses a new semantic multi-robot planning problem in uncertain and dynamic environments. Particularly, the environment is occupied with non-cooperative, mobile, uncertain labeled targets. These targets are governed by stochastic dynamics while their current and future positions as well as their semantic labels are uncertain. Our goal is to control mobile sensing robots so that they can accomplish collaborative semantic tasks defined over the uncertain current/future positions and labels of these targets. We express these tasks using Linear Temporal Logic (LTL). We propose a sampling-based approach that explores the robot motion space, the mission specification space, as well as the future configurations of the labeled targets to design optimal paths. These paths are revised online to adapt to uncertain perceptual feedback. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first work that addresses semantic mission planning problems in uncertain and dynamic semantic environments. We provide extensive experiments that demonstrate the efficiency of the proposed method
abstract_zh: 本文解决了不确定和动态环境中的新语义多机器人规划问题。特别是，环境中充满了非合作的、移动的、不确定的标记目标。这些目标受随机动力学控制，而它们当前和未来的位置以及语义标签是不确定的。我们的目标是控制移动传感机器人，以便它们能够完成在这些目标的不确定的当前/未来位置和标签上定义的协作语义任务。我们使用线性时序逻辑（LTL）来表达这些任务。我们提出了一种基于采样的方法，探索机器人运动空间、任务规范空间以及标记目标的未来配置，以设计最佳路径。这些路径会在线修改以适应不确定的感知反馈。据我们所知，这是第一个解决不确定和动态语义环境中的语义任务规划问题的工作。我们提供了大量的实验来证明所提出方法的效率

AI.tldr: 本文研究了在动态语义环境中多机器人协作完成语义任务的问题，针对目标位置和语义标签的不确定性提出规划方法。
AI.motivation: 现有方法难以应对动态环境中目标位置和语义标签的不确定性，需设计适应不确定性的多机器人协同规划框架。
AI.method: 提出基于采样的规划方法，联合探索机器人运动空间、任务规范空间及目标未来状态，结合在线反馈调整路径。
AI.result: 实验验证了方法在动态语义环境中的高效性，能够有效应对目标不确定性和语义任务需求。
AI.conclusion: 首次将语义任务规划扩展到动态不确定环境，提出的采样方法为多机器人系统提供了新的解决方案。

authors_text: Mojtaba Kolahdouzi,Alireza Sepas-Moghaddam,Ali Etemad
title: FaceTopoNet: Facial Expression Recognition using Face Topology Learning
title_zh: FaceTopoNet：使用面部拓扑学习进行面部表情识别
abstract: Prior work has shown that the order in which different components of the face are learned using a sequential learner can play an important role in the performance of facial expression recognition systems. We propose FaceTopoNet, an end-to-end deep model for facial expression recognition, which is capable of learning an effective tree topology of the face. Our model then traverses the learned tree to generate a sequence, which is then used to form an embedding to feed a sequential learner. The devised model adopts one stream for learning structure and one stream for learning texture. The structure stream focuses on the positions of the facial landmarks, while the main focus of the texture stream is on the patches around the landmarks to learn textural information. We then fuse the outputs of the two streams by utilizing an effective attention-based fusion strategy. We perform extensive experiments on four large-scale in-the-wild facial expression datasets - namely AffectNet, FER2013, ExpW, and RAF-DB - and one lab-controlled dataset (CK+) to evaluate our approach. FaceTopoNet achieves state-of-the-art performance on three of the five datasets and obtains competitive results on the other two datasets. We also perform rigorous ablation and sensitivity experiments to evaluate the impact of different components and parameters in our model. Lastly, we perform robustness experiments and demonstrate that FaceTopoNet is more robust against occlusions in comparison to other leading methods in the area.
summary: Prior work has shown that the order in which different components of the face are learned using a sequential learner can play an important role in the performance of facial expression recognition systems. We propose FaceTopoNet, an end-to-end deep model for facial expression recognition, which is capable of learning an effective tree topology of the face. Our model then traverses the learned tree to generate a sequence, which is then used to form an embedding to feed a sequential learner. The devised model adopts one stream for learning structure and one stream for learning texture. The structure stream focuses on the positions of the facial landmarks, while the main focus of the texture stream is on the patches around the landmarks to learn textural information. We then fuse the outputs of the two streams by utilizing an effective attention-based fusion strategy. We perform extensive experiments on four large-scale in-the-wild facial expression datasets - namely AffectNet, FER2013, ExpW, and RAF-DB - and one lab-controlled dataset (CK+) to evaluate our approach. FaceTopoNet achieves state-of-the-art performance on three of the five datasets and obtains competitive results on the other two datasets. We also perform rigorous ablation and sensitivity experiments to evaluate the impact of different components and parameters in our model. Lastly, we perform robustness experiments and demonstrate that FaceTopoNet is more robust against occlusions in comparison to other leading methods in the area.
abstract_zh: 先前的工作表明，使用顺序学习器学习面部不同组成部分的顺序可以在面部表情识别系统的性能中发挥重要作用。我们提出了 FaceTopoNet，一种用于面部表情识别的端到端深度模型，它能够学习面部的有效树拓扑。然后，我们的模型遍历学习树以生成序列，然后使用该序列形成嵌入以提供顺序学习器。所设计的模型采用一种流学习结构和一种流学习纹理。结构流关注面部标志的位置，而纹理流主要关注标志周围的补丁以学习纹理信息。然后，我们利用有效的基于注意力的融合策略来融合两个流的输出。我们对四个大型野外面部表情数据集（即 AffectNet、FER2013、ExpW 和 RAF-DB）以及一个实验室控制数据集 (CK+) 进行了广泛的实验，以评估我们的方法。 FaceTopoNet 在五个数据集中的三个上实现了最先进的性能，并在其他两个数据集上获得了有竞争力的结果。我们还进行严格的消融和敏感性实验，以评估模型中不同组件和参数的影响。最后，我们进行了鲁棒性实验，并证明与该领域的其他领先方法相比，FaceTopoNet 对遮挡的鲁棒性更强。

AI.tldr: 本文提出FaceTopoNet模型，通过学习面部拓扑结构并结合纹理信息，提升面部表情识别的性能。
AI.motivation: 现有研究发现面部组件的学习顺序影响表情识别效果，但如何有效建模面部结构与纹理关系仍需改进。
AI.method: 模型包含结构流（学习面部标志点位置）和纹理流（学习标志点周围纹理），通过注意力机制融合特征，并生成拓扑序列嵌入以提升顺序学习效果。
AI.result: 在AffectNet、FER2013、ExpW、RAF-DB和CK+五个数据集上，FaceTopoNet在三个数据集上达到SOTA，其他两个数据集表现竞争力，且对遮挡具有更强鲁棒性。
AI.conclusion: FaceTopoNet通过拓扑结构与纹理的联合建模有效提升了表情识别性能，验证了拓扑学习与注意力融合方法的有效性。

search_text: FaceTopoNet: Facial Expression Recognition using Face Topology Learning FaceTopoNet：使用面部拓扑学习进行面部表情识别 Prior work has shown that the order in which different components of the face are learned using a sequential learner can play an important role in the performance of facial expression recognition systems. We propose FaceTopoNet, an end-to-end deep model for facial expression recognition, which is capable of learning an effective tree topology of the face. Our model then traverses the learned tree to generate a sequence, which is then used to form an embedding to feed a sequential learner. The devised model adopts one stream for learning structure and one stream for learning texture. The structure stream focuses on the positions of the facial landmarks, while the main focus of the texture stream is on the patches around the landmarks to learn textural information. We then fuse the outputs of the two streams by utilizing an effective attention-based fusion strategy. We perform extensive experiments on four large-scale in-the-wild facial expression datasets - namely AffectNet, FER2013, ExpW, and RAF-DB - and one lab-controlled dataset (CK+) to evaluate our approach. FaceTopoNet achieves state-of-the-art performance on three of the five datasets and obtains competitive results on the other two datasets. We also perform rigorous ablation and sensitivity experiments to evaluate the impact of different components and parameters in our model. Lastly, we perform robustness experiments and demonstrate that FaceTopoNet is more robust against occlusions in comparison to other leading methods in the area. 先前的工作表明，使用顺序学习器学习面部不同组成部分的顺序可以在面部表情识别系统的性能中发挥重要作用。我们提出了 FaceTopoNet，一种用于面部表情识别的端到端深度模型，它能够学习面部的有效树拓扑。然后，我们的模型遍历学习树以生成序列，然后使用该序列形成嵌入以提供顺序学习器。所设计的模型采用一种流学习结构和一种流学习纹理。结构流关注面部标志的位置，而纹理流主要关注标志周围的补丁以学习纹理信息。然后，我们利用有效的基于注意力的融合策略来融合两个流的输出。我们对四个大型野外面部表情数据集（即 AffectNet、FER2013、ExpW 和 RAF-DB）以及一个实验室控制数据集 (CK+) 进行了广泛的实验，以评估我们的方法。 FaceTopoNet 在五个数据集中的三个上实现了最先进的性能，并在其他两个数据集上获得了有竞争力的结果。我们还进行严格的消融和敏感性实验，以评估模型中不同组件和参数的影响。最后，我们进行了鲁棒性实验，并证明与该领域的其他领先方法相比，FaceTopoNet 对遮挡的鲁棒性更强。 本文提出FaceTopoNet模型，通过学习面部拓扑结构并结合纹理信息，提升面部表情识别的性能。 Mojtaba Kolahdouzi,Alireza Sepas-Moghaddam,Ali Etemad cs.CV

authors_text: Anna Gottardi,Osman Ipek,Giuseppe Castellucci,Shui Hu,Lavina Vaz,Yao Lu,Anju Khatri,Anjali Chadha,Desheng Zhang,Sattvik Sahai,Prerna Dwivedi,Hangjie Shi,Lucy Hu,Andy Huang,Luke Dai,Bofei Yang,Varun Somani,Pankaj Rajan,Ron Rezac,Michael Johnston,Savanna Stiff,Leslie Ball,David Carmel,Yang Liu,Dilek Hakkani-Tur, et al. title: Alexa, Let's Work Together: Introducing the First Alexa Prize TaskBot Challenge on Conversational Task Assistance
title_zh: Alexa，让我们一起努力：推出首届 Alexa 奖 TaskBot 对话任务协助挑战赛
comment: 14 pages, Proceedings of Alexa Prize Taskbot (Alexa Prize 2021)
comments: 14 pages, Proceedings of Alexa Prize Taskbot (Alexa Prize 2021)
abstract: Since its inception in 2016, the Alexa Prize program has enabled hundreds of university students to explore and compete to develop conversational agents through the SocialBot Grand Challenge. The goal of the challenge is to build agents capable of conversing coherently and engagingly with humans on popular topics for 20 minutes, while achieving an average rating of at least 4.0/5.0. However, as conversational agents attempt to assist users with increasingly complex tasks, new conversational AI techniques and evaluation platforms are needed. The Alexa Prize TaskBot challenge, established in 2021, builds on the success of the SocialBot challenge by introducing the requirements of interactively assisting humans with real-world Cooking and Do-It-Yourself tasks, while making use of both voice and visual modalities. This challenge requires the TaskBots to identify and understand the user's need, identify and integrate task and domain knowledge into the interaction, and develop new ways of engaging the user without distracting them from the task at hand, among other challenges. This paper provides an overview of the TaskBot challenge, describes the infrastructure support provided to the teams with the CoBot Toolkit, and summarizes the approaches the participating teams took to overcome the research challenges. Finally, it analyzes the performance of the competing TaskBots during the first year of the competition.
summary: Since its inception in 2016, the Alexa Prize program has enabled hundreds of university students to explore and compete to develop conversational agents through the SocialBot Grand Challenge. The goal of the challenge is to build agents capable of conversing coherently and engagingly with humans on popular topics for 20 minutes, while achieving an average rating of at least 4.0/5.0. However, as conversational agents attempt to assist users with increasingly complex tasks, new conversational AI techniques and evaluation platforms are needed. The Alexa Prize TaskBot challenge, established in 2021, builds on the success of the SocialBot challenge by introducing the requirements of interactively assisting humans with real-world Cooking and Do-It-Yourself tasks, while making use of both voice and visual modalities. This challenge requires the TaskBots to identify and understand the user's need, identify and integrate task and domain knowledge into the interaction, and develop new ways of engaging the user without distracting them from the task at hand, among other challenges. This paper provides an overview of the TaskBot challenge, describes the infrastructure support provided to the teams with the CoBot Toolkit, and summarizes the approaches the participating teams took to overcome the research challenges. Finally, it analyzes the performance of the competing TaskBots during the first year of the competition.
abstract_zh: 自 2016 年启动以来，Alexa 奖计划已让数百名大学生通过 SocialBot Grand Challenge 探索和竞争开发对话代理。该挑战的目标是构建能够与人类就热门话题连贯且互动地对话 20 分钟的智能体，同时获得至少 4.0/5.0 的平均评分。然而，随着对话代理试图帮助用户完成日益复杂的任务，需要新的对话人工智能技术和评估平台。 Alexa 奖 TaskBot 挑战赛于 2021 年设立，以 SocialBot 挑战赛的成功为基础，引入了交互式协助人类完成现实世界烹饪和自己动手任务的要求，同时利用语音和视觉方式。除其他挑战外，这一挑战要求 TaskBot 识别和理解用户的需求，识别任务和领域知识并将其集成到交互中，并开发吸引用户的新方法而不分散他们对手头任务的注意力。本文概述了 TaskBot 挑战，描述了 CoBot 工具包为团队提供的基础设施支持，并总结了参与团队为克服研究挑战所采取的方法。最后，它分析了竞争任务机器人在比赛第一年的表现。

AI.tldr: 本文介绍了Alexa Prize TaskBot挑战赛，旨在开发能够通过语音和视觉模态协助用户完成烹饪和DIY任务的对话代理。
AI.motivation: 随着对话代理需处理日益复杂的任务，传统社交对话挑战无法满足需求，需新方法和评估平台支持任务导向的交互。
AI.method: 通过CoBot工具包提供基础设施支持，要求参赛团队开发能理解用户需求、整合领域知识并保持用户专注的TaskBot。
AI.result: 分析了首年竞赛中TaskBot的表现，总结了团队采用的多模态交互、任务规划和自然语言理解等关键技术方案。
AI.conclusion: TaskBot挑战赛推动了任务辅助对话代理的研究，验证了多模态交互的有效性，同时指出了领域知识整合和上下文理解的持续挑战。

authors_text: David Piorkowski,Michael Hind,John Richards
title: Quantitative AI Risk Assessments: Opportunities and Challenges
title_zh: 定量人工智能风险评估：机遇与挑战
comment: This is a draft of a forthcoming article to appear in the Seton Hall Journal of Legislation and Public Policy
comments: This is a draft of a forthcoming article to appear in the Seton Hall Journal of Legislation and Public Policy
abstract: Although AI systems are increasingly being leveraged to provide value to organizations, individuals, and society, significant attendant risks have been identified and have manifested. These risks have led to proposed regulations, litigation, and general societal concerns. As with any promising technology, organizations want to benefit from the positive capabilities of AI technology while reducing the risks. The best way to reduce risks is to implement comprehensive AI lifecycle governance where policies and procedures are described and enforced during the design, development, deployment, and monitoring of an AI system. Although support for comprehensive governance is beginning to emerge, organizations often need to identify the risks of deploying an already-built model without knowledge of how it was constructed or access to its original developers. Such an assessment will quantitatively assess the risks of an existing model in a manner analogous to how a home inspector might assess the risks of an already-built home or a physician might assess overall patient health based on a battery of tests. Several AI risks can be quantified using metrics from the technical community. However, there are numerous issues in deciding how these metrics can be leveraged to create a quantitative AI risk assessment. This paper explores these issues, focusing on the opportunities, challenges, and potential impacts of such an approach, and discussing how it might influence AI regulations.
summary: Although AI systems are increasingly being leveraged to provide value to organizations, individuals, and society, significant attendant risks have been identified and have manifested. These risks have led to proposed regulations, litigation, and general societal concerns. As with any promising technology, organizations want to benefit from the positive capabilities of AI technology while reducing the risks. The best way to reduce risks is to implement comprehensive AI lifecycle governance where policies and procedures are described and enforced during the design, development, deployment, and monitoring of an AI system. Although support for comprehensive governance is beginning to emerge, organizations often need to identify the risks of deploying an already-built model without knowledge of how it was constructed or access to its original developers. Such an assessment will quantitatively assess the risks of an existing model in a manner analogous to how a home inspector might assess the risks of an already-built home or a physician might assess overall patient health based on a battery of tests. Several AI risks can be quantified using metrics from the technical community. However, there are numerous issues in deciding how these metrics can be leveraged to create a quantitative AI risk assessment. This paper explores these issues, focusing on the opportunities, challenges, and potential impacts of such an approach, and discussing how it might influence AI regulations.
abstract_zh: 尽管人工智能系统越来越多地被用来为组织、个人和社会提供价值，但随之而来的重大风险已经被发现并显现出来。这些风险导致了拟议的法规、诉讼和普遍的社会关注。与任何有前景的技术一样，组织希望从人工智能技术的积极功能中受益，同时降低风险。降低风险的最佳方法是实施全面的人工智能生命周期治理，其中在人工智能系统的设计、开发、部署和监控过程中描述和执行政策和程序。尽管对全面治理的支持开始出现，但组织通常需要识别部署已构建模型的风险，而不了解模型的构建方式或接触其原始开发人员的方式。这种评估将以类似于房屋检查员评估已建成房屋的风险或医生根据一系列测试评估患者整体健康状况的方式定量评估现有模型的风险。可以使用技术社区的指标来量化多种人工智能风险。然而，在决定如何利用这些指标来创建定量人工智能风险评估时存在许多问题。本文探讨了这些问题，重点关注这种方法的机遇、挑战和潜在影响，并讨论它如何影响人工智能法规。

AI.tldr: 本文探讨了对现有AI模型进行量化风险评估的机会、挑战及潜在影响，类似于房屋检查或医疗检测的方式。
AI.motivation: 尽管AI系统带来价值，但其风险引发监管、诉讼和社会担忧，组织需在无法获取模型构建信息的情况下评估已部署模型的风险。
AI.method: 通过分析技术社区的量化指标，探讨如何将其应用于AI风险评估框架，并讨论该方法的可行性与挑战。
AI.result: 识别出量化AI风险评估的潜在机会与挑战，包括指标选择、评估标准不统一及对监管政策的影响。
AI.conclusion: 量化风险评估为AI治理提供新思路，但需解决指标整合与评估方法标准化问题，以推动更有效的监管框架。

tldr: 本文探讨了对现有AI模型进行量化风险评估的机会、挑战及潜在影响，类似于房屋检查或医疗检测的方式。
motivation: 尽管AI系统带来价值，但其风险引发监管、诉讼和社会担忧，组织需在无法获取模型构建信息的情况下评估已部署模型的风险。
method: 通过分析技术社区的量化指标，探讨如何将其应用于AI风险评估框架，并讨论该方法的可行性与挑战。
result: 识别出量化AI风险评估的潜在机会与挑战，包括指标选择、评估标准不统一及对监管政策的影响。
conclusion: 量化风险评估为AI治理提供新思路，但需解决指标整合与评估方法标准化问题，以推动更有效的监管框架。
search_text: Quantitative AI Risk Assessments: Opportunities and Challenges 定量人工智能风险评估：机遇与挑战 Although AI systems are increasingly being leveraged to provide value to organizations, individuals, and society, significant attendant risks have been identified and have manifested. These risks have led to proposed regulations, litigation, and general societal concerns. As with any promising technology, organizations want to benefit from the positive capabilities of AI technology while reducing the risks. The best way to reduce risks is to implement comprehensive AI lifecycle governance where policies and procedures are described and enforced during the design, development, deployment, and monitoring of an AI system. Although support for comprehensive governance is beginning to emerge, organizations often need to identify the risks of deploying an already-built model without knowledge of how it was constructed or access to its original developers. Such an assessment will quantitatively assess the risks of an existing model in a manner analogous to how a home inspector might assess the risks of an already-built home or a physician might assess overall patient health based on a battery of tests. Several AI risks can be quantified using metrics from the technical community. However, there are numerous issues in deciding how these metrics can be leveraged to create a quantitative AI risk assessment. This paper explores these issues, focusing on the opportunities, challenges, and potential impacts of such an approach, and discussing how it might influence AI regulations. 尽管人工智能系统越来越多地被用来为组织、个人和社会提供价值，但随之而来的重大风险已经被发现并显现出来。这些风险导致了拟议的法规、诉讼和普遍的社会关注。与任何有前景的技术一样，组织希望从人工智能技术的积极功能中受益，同时降低风险。降低风险的最佳方法是实施全面的人工智能生命周期治理，其中在人工智能系统的设计、开发、部署和监控过程中描述和执行政策和程序。尽管对全面治理的支持开始出现，但组织通常需要识别部署已构建模型的风险，而不了解模型的构建方式或接触其原始开发人员的方式。这种评估将以类似于房屋检查员评估已建成房屋的风险或医生根据一系列测试评估患者整体健康状况的方式定量评估现有模型的风险。可以使用技术社区的指标来量化多种人工智能风险。然而，在决定如何利用这些指标来创建定量人工智能风险评估时存在许多问题。本文探讨了这些问题，重点关注这种方法的机遇、挑战和潜在影响，并讨论它如何影响人工智能法规。 本文探讨了对现有AI模型进行量化风险评估的机会、挑战及潜在影响，类似于房屋检查或医疗检测的方式。 David Piorkowski,Michael Hind,John Richards cs.AI

authors_text: Islam Ali,Hong Zhang
title: Optimizing SLAM Evaluation Footprint Through Dynamic Range Coverage Analysis of Datasets
title_zh: 通过数据集的动态范围覆盖分析优化 SLAM 评估足迹
abstract: Simultaneous Localization and Mapping (SLAM) is considered an ever-evolving problem due to its usage in many applications. Evaluation of SLAM is done typically using publicly available datasets which are increasing in number and the level of difficulty. Each dataset provides a certain level of dynamic range coverage that is a key aspect of measuring the robustness and resilience of SLAM. In this paper, we provide a systematic analysis of the dynamic range coverage of datasets based on a number of characterization metrics, and our analysis shows a huge level of redundancy within and between datasets. Subsequently, we propose a dynamic programming (DP) algorithm for eliminating the redundancy in the evaluation process of SLAM by selecting a subset of sequences that matches a single or multiple dynamic range coverage objectives. It is shown that, with the help of dataset characterization and DP selection algorithm, a reduction in the evaluation effort can be achieved while maintaining the same level of coverage. We also study how the evaluation process of a real-world SLAM system can be optimized utilizing the method proposed.
summary: Simultaneous Localization and Mapping (SLAM) is considered an ever-evolving problem due to its usage in many applications. Evaluation of SLAM is done typically using publicly available datasets which are increasing in number and the level of difficulty. Each dataset provides a certain level of dynamic range coverage that is a key aspect of measuring the robustness and resilience of SLAM. In this paper, we provide a systematic analysis of the dynamic range coverage of datasets based on a number of characterization metrics, and our analysis shows a huge level of redundancy within and between datasets. Subsequently, we propose a dynamic programming (DP) algorithm for eliminating the redundancy in the evaluation process of SLAM by selecting a subset of sequences that matches a single or multiple dynamic range coverage objectives. It is shown that, with the help of dataset characterization and DP selection algorithm, a reduction in the evaluation effort can be achieved while maintaining the same level of coverage. We also study how the evaluation process of a real-world SLAM system can be optimized utilizing the method proposed.
abstract_zh: 由于同步定位和建图 (SLAM) 在许多应用中的使用，它被认为是一个不断发展的问题。 SLAM 的评估通常使用公开的数据集来完成，这些数据集的数量和难度都在不断增加。每个数据集都提供一定程度的动态范围覆盖，这是衡量 SLAM 稳健性和弹性的关键方面。在本文中，我们基于许多特征指标对数据集的动态范围覆盖进行了系统分析，我们的分析显示了数据集内部和数据集之间存在巨大的冗余。随后，我们提出了一种动态规划（DP）算法，通过选择与单个或多个动态范围覆盖目标匹配的序列子集来消除 SLAM 评估过程中的冗余。结果表明，借助数据集表征和 DP 选择算法，可以在保持相同覆盖水平的同时减少评估工作。我们还研究了如何利用所提出的方法来优化现实世界 SLAM 系统的评估过程。

AI.tldr: 通过动态范围覆盖率分析优化SLAM评估数据集的冗余问题
AI.motivation: 现有SLAM评估数据集中存在大量冗余，导致评估效率低下且无法有效覆盖动态范围需求
AI.method: 提出基于动态规划算法的序列选择方法，通过数据集特征化分析消除冗余
AI.result: 在保持相同覆盖率的前提下，成功减少SLAM系统评估所需的数据集规模
AI.conclusion: 所提方法能有效优化实际SLAM系统的评估流程，提升资源利用效率

authors_text: James F. Mullen Jr,Divya Kothandaraman,Aniket Bera,Dinesh Manocha
title: Placing Human Animations into 3D Scenes by Learning Interaction- and Geometry-Driven Keyframes
title_zh: 通过学习交互和几何驱动的关键帧将人体动画放入 3D 场景中
comment: WACV 2023. Our project website is available at https://gamma.umd.edu/paak/
comments: WACV 2023. Our project website is available at https://gamma.umd.edu/paak/
abstract: We present a novel method for placing a 3D human animation into a 3D scene while maintaining any human-scene interactions in the animation. We use the notion of computing the most important meshes in the animation for the interaction with the scene, which we call "keyframes." These keyframes allow us to better optimize the placement of the animation into the scene such that interactions in the animations (standing, laying, sitting, etc.) match the affordances of the scene (e.g., standing on the floor or laying in a bed). We compare our method, which we call PAAK, with prior approaches, including POSA, PROX ground truth, and a motion synthesis method, and highlight the benefits of our method with a perceptual study. Human raters preferred our PAAK method over the PROX ground truth data 64.6\% of the time. Additionally, in direct comparisons, the raters preferred PAAK over competing methods including 61.5\% compared to POSA.
summary: We present a novel method for placing a 3D human animation into a 3D scene while maintaining any human-scene interactions in the animation. We use the notion of computing the most important meshes in the animation for the interaction with the scene, which we call "keyframes." These keyframes allow us to better optimize the placement of the animation into the scene such that interactions in the animations (standing, laying, sitting, etc.) match the affordances of the scene (e.g., standing on the floor or laying in a bed). We compare our method, which we call PAAK, with prior approaches, including POSA, PROX ground truth, and a motion synthesis method, and highlight the benefits of our method with a perceptual study. Human raters preferred our PAAK method over the PROX ground truth data 64.6\% of the time. Additionally, in direct comparisons, the raters preferred PAAK over competing methods including 61.5\% compared to POSA.
abstract_zh: 我们提出了一种新颖的方法，用于将 3D 人类动画放入 3D 场景中，同时保持动画中的任何人类场景交互。我们使用计算动画中最重要的网格的概念来与场景交互，我们将其称为“关键帧”。这些关键帧使我们能够更好地优化动画在场景中的放置，以便动画中的交互（站立、躺着、坐着等）与场景的可供性（例如，站在地板上或躺在床上）相匹配。我们将我们称为 PAAK 的方法与之前的方法（包括 POSA、PROX 地面实况和运动合成方法）进行比较，并通过感知研究强调我们方法的优点。相比 PROX 真实数据，人类评分者在 64.6% 的时间里更喜欢我们的 PAAK 方法。此外，在直接比较中，与竞争方法相比，评价者更喜欢 PAAK，其中与 POSA 相比，评价者更喜欢 PAAK，其中 61.5%。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种将3D人体动画嵌入3D场景的新方法，确保动画中的人与场景的交互保持一致。
AI.motivation: 现有方法在将人体动画放置到场景时难以保持动画中的人与场景的交互（如站立、躺卧等）。
AI.method: 通过计算动画中与场景交互的关键帧（keyframes），结合交互和几何信息优化动画放置，提出名为PAAK的方法。
AI.result: 感知实验显示，人类评分者64.6%的情况下更偏好PAAK方法，且优于其他方法（如POSA）。
AI.conclusion: PAAK方法通过考虑交互与几何信息，有效提升了人体动画在场景中的放置效果。

authors_text: Ahmad Bilal Asghar,Guangyao Shi,Nare Karapetyan,James Humann,Jean-Paul Reddinger,James Dotterweich,Pratap Tokekar
title: Risk-aware Resource Allocation for Multiple UAVs-UGVs Recharging Rendezvous
title_zh: 多无人机-UGV 充电交会的风险感知资源分配
abstract: We study a resource allocation problem for the cooperative aerial-ground vehicle routing application, in which multiple Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) with limited battery capacity and multiple Unmanned Ground Vehicles (UGVs) that can also act as a mobile recharging stations need to jointly accomplish a mission such as persistently monitoring a set of points. Due to the limited battery capacity of the UAVs, they sometimes have to deviate from their task to rendezvous with the UGVs and get recharged. Each UGV can serve a limited number of UAVs at a time. In contrast to prior work on deterministic multi-robot scheduling, we consider the challenge imposed by the stochasticity of the energy consumption of the UAV. We are interested in finding the optimal recharging schedule of the UAVs such that the travel cost is minimized and the probability that no UAV runs out of charge within the planning horizon is greater than a user-defined tolerance. We formulate this problem ({Risk-aware Recharging Rendezvous Problem (RRRP))} as an Integer Linear Program (ILP), in which the matching constraint captures the resource availability constraints and the knapsack constraint captures the success probability constraints. We propose a bicriteria approximation algorithm to solve RRRP. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our formulation and algorithm in the context of one persistent monitoring mission.
summary: We study a resource allocation problem for the cooperative aerial-ground vehicle routing application, in which multiple Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) with limited battery capacity and multiple Unmanned Ground Vehicles (UGVs) that can also act as a mobile recharging stations need to jointly accomplish a mission such as persistently monitoring a set of points. Due to the limited battery capacity of the UAVs, they sometimes have to deviate from their task to rendezvous with the UGVs and get recharged. Each UGV can serve a limited number of UAVs at a time. In contrast to prior work on deterministic multi-robot scheduling, we consider the challenge imposed by the stochasticity of the energy consumption of the UAV. We are interested in finding the optimal recharging schedule of the UAVs such that the travel cost is minimized and the probability that no UAV runs out of charge within the planning horizon is greater than a user-defined tolerance. We formulate this problem ({Risk-aware Recharging Rendezvous Problem (RRRP))} as an Integer Linear Program (ILP), in which the matching constraint captures the resource availability constraints and the knapsack constraint captures the success probability constraints. We propose a bicriteria approximation algorithm to solve RRRP. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our formulation and algorithm in the context of one persistent monitoring mission.
abstract_zh: 我们研究了协作式空中地面车辆路由应用的资源分配问题，其中多个电池容量有限的无人机（UAV）和多个也可以充当移动充电站的无人地面车辆（UGV）需要共同完成诸如持续监控一组点之类的任务。由于无人机的电池容量有限，它们有时不得不偏离任务去与无人地面车辆交会并充电。每辆 UGV 一次只能为有限数量的无人机提供服务。与先前关于确定性多机器人调度的工作相比，我们考虑了无人机能耗的随机性带来的挑战。我们感兴趣的是找到无人机的最佳充电时间表，以使旅行成本最小化，并且在规划范围内没有无人机耗尽电量的概率大于用户定义的容差。我们将这个问题（{风险感知充电集合问题（RRRP））}表述为整数线性规划（ILP），其中匹配约束捕获资源可用性约束，背包约束捕获成功概率约束。我们提出了一种双标准近似算法来求解 RRRP。我们在一项持续监测任务的背景下展示了我们的公式和算法的有效性。

AI.tldr: 本文研究多无人机（UAV）与地面无人车（UGV）协同执行任务时的资源分配问题，重点解决UAV电池有限需与UGV会合充电的调度优化。
AI.motivation: UAV电池容量有限需频繁充电，而UGV可作为移动充电站。现有研究多基于确定性模型，未考虑UAV能耗的随机性，导致实际应用中存在任务失败风险。
AI.method: 将问题建模为整数线性规划（ILP），引入匹配约束（资源可用性）和背包约束（成功概率），并设计双目标近似算法求解。
AI.result: 通过持续监控任务实验验证了模型与算法的有效性，能够在保证UAV电量不足概率低于阈值的同时最小化行驶成本。
AI.conclusion: 该方法有效应对了能耗随机性与资源约束的挑战，为多机协同持久任务提供了风险可控的调度方案。

search_text: Risk-aware Resource Allocation for Multiple UAVs-UGVs Recharging Rendezvous 多无人机-UGV 充电交会的风险感知资源分配 We study a resource allocation problem for the cooperative aerial-ground vehicle routing application, in which multiple Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) with limited battery capacity and multiple Unmanned Ground Vehicles (UGVs) that can also act as a mobile recharging stations need to jointly accomplish a mission such as persistently monitoring a set of points. Due to the limited battery capacity of the UAVs, they sometimes have to deviate from their task to rendezvous with the UGVs and get recharged. Each UGV can serve a limited number of UAVs at a time. In contrast to prior work on deterministic multi-robot scheduling, we consider the challenge imposed by the stochasticity of the energy consumption of the UAV. We are interested in finding the optimal recharging schedule of the UAVs such that the travel cost is minimized and the probability that no UAV runs out of charge within the planning horizon is greater than a user-defined tolerance. We formulate this problem ({Risk-aware Recharging Rendezvous Problem (RRRP))} as an Integer Linear Program (ILP), in which the matching constraint captures the resource availability constraints and the knapsack constraint captures the success probability constraints. We propose a bicriteria approximation algorithm to solve RRRP. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our formulation and algorithm in the context of one persistent monitoring mission. 我们研究了协作式空中地面车辆路由应用的资源分配问题，其中多个电池容量有限的无人机（UAV）和多个也可以充当移动充电站的无人地面车辆（UGV）需要共同完成诸如持续监控一组点之类的任务。由于无人机的电池容量有限，它们有时不得不偏离任务去与无人地面车辆交会并充电。每辆 UGV 一次只能为有限数量的无人机提供服务。与先前关于确定性多机器人调度的工作相比，我们考虑了无人机能耗的随机性带来的挑战。我们感兴趣的是找到无人机的最佳充电时间表，以使旅行成本最小化，并且在规划范围内没有无人机耗尽电量的概率大于用户定义的容差。我们将这个问题（{风险感知充电集合问题（RRRP））}表述为整数线性规划（ILP），其中匹配约束捕获资源可用性约束，背包约束捕获成功概率约束。我们提出了一种双标准近似算法来求解 RRRP。我们在一项持续监测任务的背景下展示了我们的公式和算法的有效性。 本文研究多无人机（UAV）与地面无人车（UGV）协同执行任务时的资源分配问题，重点解决UAV电池有限需与UGV会合充电的调度优化。 Ahmad Bilal Asghar,Guangyao Shi,Nare Karapetyan,James Humann,Jean-Paul Reddinger,James Dotterweich,Pratap Tokekar cs.RO

authors_text: William Hackett,Stefan Trawicki,Zhengxin Yu,Neeraj Suri,Peter Garraghan
title: PINCH: An Adversarial Extraction Attack Framework for Deep Learning Models
title_zh: PINCH：深度学习模型的对抗性提取攻击框架
comment: 19 pages, 13 figures, 5 tables
comments: 19 pages, 13 figures, 5 tables
abstract: Adversarial extraction attacks constitute an insidious threat against Deep Learning (DL) models in-which an adversary aims to steal the architecture, parameters, and hyper-parameters of a targeted DL model. Existing extraction attack literature have observed varying levels of attack success for different DL models and datasets, yet the underlying cause(s) behind their susceptibility often remain unclear, and would help facilitate creating secure DL systems. In this paper we present PINCH: an efficient and automated extraction attack framework capable of designing, deploying, and analyzing extraction attack scenarios across heterogeneous hardware platforms. Using PINCH, we perform extensive experimental evaluation of extraction attacks against 21 model architectures to explore new extraction attack scenarios and further attack staging. Our findings show (1) key extraction characteristics whereby particular model configurations exhibit strong resilience against specific attacks, (2) even partial extraction success enables further staging for other adversarial attacks, and (3) equivalent stolen models uncover differences in expressive power, yet exhibit similar captured knowledge.
summary: Adversarial extraction attacks constitute an insidious threat against Deep Learning (DL) models in-which an adversary aims to steal the architecture, parameters, and hyper-parameters of a targeted DL model. Existing extraction attack literature have observed varying levels of attack success for different DL models and datasets, yet the underlying cause(s) behind their susceptibility often remain unclear, and would help facilitate creating secure DL systems. In this paper we present PINCH: an efficient and automated extraction attack framework capable of designing, deploying, and analyzing extraction attack scenarios across heterogeneous hardware platforms. Using PINCH, we perform extensive experimental evaluation of extraction attacks against 21 model architectures to explore new extraction attack scenarios and further attack staging. Our findings show (1) key extraction characteristics whereby particular model configurations exhibit strong resilience against specific attacks, (2) even partial extraction success enables further staging for other adversarial attacks, and (3) equivalent stolen models uncover differences in expressive power, yet exhibit similar captured knowledge.
abstract_zh: 对抗性提取攻击对深度学习 (DL) 模型构成了潜在的威胁，其中攻击者旨在窃取目标 DL 模型的架构、参数和超参数。现有的提取攻击文献已经观察到不同深度学习模型和数据集的攻击成功程度不同，但其易感性背后的根本原因通常仍不清楚，这将有助于促进创建安全的深度学习系统。在本文中，我们提出了 PINCH：一种高效、自动化的提取攻击框架，能够跨异构硬件平台设计、部署和分析提取攻击场景。使用 PINCH，我们对 21 种模型架构的提取攻击进行了广泛的实验评估，以探索新的提取攻击场景和进一步的攻击阶段。我们的研究结果表明（1）关键提取特征，其中特定模型配置对特定攻击表现出强大的弹性，（2）即使部分提取成功也可以进一步进行其他对抗性攻击，（3）等效的被盗模型揭示了表达能力的差异，但表现出相似的捕获知识。

AI.tldr: 本文提出PINCH框架，用于通过对抗性提取攻击窃取深度学习模型的架构、参数和超参数，并分析不同模型的易受攻击性。
AI.motivation: 现有提取攻击对不同模型和数据集的成功率差异显著，但其背后的原因尚不明确，需通过系统性研究提升深度学习系统安全性。
AI.method: 开发自动化框架PINCH，在异构硬件平台上设计、部署和分析提取攻击场景，对21种模型架构进行大规模实验评估。
AI.result: 发现特定模型配置对特定攻击具有强抗性；部分提取成功可为其他攻击提供基础；窃取模型表达能力差异但知识相似。
AI.conclusion: PINCH揭示了模型易受攻击的内在特性，强调需针对部分提取风险设计防御机制，并推动更安全的深度学习系统开发。

search_text: PINCH: An Adversarial Extraction Attack Framework for Deep Learning Models PINCH：深度学习模型的对抗性提取攻击框架 Adversarial extraction attacks constitute an insidious threat against Deep Learning (DL) models in-which an adversary aims to steal the architecture, parameters, and hyper-parameters of a targeted DL model. Existing extraction attack literature have observed varying levels of attack success for different DL models and datasets, yet the underlying cause(s) behind their susceptibility often remain unclear, and would help facilitate creating secure DL systems. In this paper we present PINCH: an efficient and automated extraction attack framework capable of designing, deploying, and analyzing extraction attack scenarios across heterogeneous hardware platforms. Using PINCH, we perform extensive experimental evaluation of extraction attacks against 21 model architectures to explore new extraction attack scenarios and further attack staging. Our findings show (1) key extraction characteristics whereby particular model configurations exhibit strong resilience against specific attacks, (2) even partial extraction success enables further staging for other adversarial attacks, and (3) equivalent stolen models uncover differences in expressive power, yet exhibit similar captured knowledge. 对抗性提取攻击对深度学习 (DL) 模型构成了潜在的威胁，其中攻击者旨在窃取目标 DL 模型的架构、参数和超参数。现有的提取攻击文献已经观察到不同深度学习模型和数据集的攻击成功程度不同，但其易感性背后的根本原因通常仍不清楚，这将有助于促进创建安全的深度学习系统。在本文中，我们提出了 PINCH：一种高效、自动化的提取攻击框架，能够跨异构硬件平台设计、部署和分析提取攻击场景。使用 PINCH，我们对 21 种模型架构的提取攻击进行了广泛的实验评估，以探索新的提取攻击场景和进一步的攻击阶段。我们的研究结果表明（1）关键提取特征，其中特定模型配置对特定攻击表现出强大的弹性，（2）即使部分提取成功也可以进一步进行其他对抗性攻击，（3）等效的被盗模型揭示了表达能力的差异，但表现出相似的捕获知识。 本文提出PINCH框架，用于通过对抗性提取攻击窃取深度学习模型的架构、参数和超参数，并分析不同模型的易受攻击性。 William Hackett,Stefan Trawicki,Zhengxin Yu,Neeraj Suri,Peter Garraghan cs.CR,cs.AI,cs.LG

authors_text: Jack Hessel,Ana Marasović,Jena D. Hwang,Lillian Lee,Jeff Da,Rowan Zellers,Robert Mankoff,Yejin Choi
title: Do Androids Laugh at Electric Sheep? Humor "Understanding" Benchmarks from The New Yorker Caption Contest
title_zh: 机器人会嘲笑电子羊吗？ 《纽约客》字幕大赛的幽默“理解”基准
comment: ef:ACL 2023
comments: ef:ACL 2023
abstract: Large neural networks can now generate jokes, but do they really "understand" humor? We challenge AI models with three tasks derived from the New Yorker Cartoon Caption Contest: matching a joke to a cartoon, identifying a winning caption, and explaining why a winning caption is funny. These tasks encapsulate progressively more sophisticated aspects of "understanding" a cartoon; key elements are the complex, often surprising relationships between images and captions and the frequent inclusion of indirect and playful allusions to human experience and culture. We investigate both multimodal and language-only models: the former are challenged with the cartoon images directly, while the latter are given multifaceted descriptions of the visual scene to simulate human-level visual understanding. We find that both types of models struggle at all three tasks. For example, our best multimodal models fall 30 accuracy points behind human performance on the matching task, and, even when provided ground-truth visual scene descriptors, human-authored explanations are preferred head-to-head over the best machine-authored ones (few-shot GPT-4) in more than 2/3 of cases. We release models, code, leaderboard, and corpus, which includes newly-gathered annotations describing the image's locations/entities, what's unusual in the scene, and an explanation of the joke.
summary: Large neural networks can now generate jokes, but do they really "understand" humor? We challenge AI models with three tasks derived from the New Yorker Cartoon Caption Contest: matching a joke to a cartoon, identifying a winning caption, and explaining why a winning caption is funny. These tasks encapsulate progressively more sophisticated aspects of "understanding" a cartoon; key elements are the complex, often surprising relationships between images and captions and the frequent inclusion of indirect and playful allusions to human experience and culture. We investigate both multimodal and language-only models: the former are challenged with the cartoon images directly, while the latter are given multifaceted descriptions of the visual scene to simulate human-level visual understanding. We find that both types of models struggle at all three tasks. For example, our best multimodal models fall 30 accuracy points behind human performance on the matching task, and, even when provided ground-truth visual scene descriptors, human-authored explanations are preferred head-to-head over the best machine-authored ones (few-shot GPT-4) in more than 2/3 of cases. We release models, code, leaderboard, and corpus, which includes newly-gathered annotations describing the image's locations/entities, what's unusual in the scene, and an explanation of the joke.
abstract_zh: 大型神经网络现在可以生成笑话，但它们真的“理解”幽默吗？我们用源自《纽约客》卡通字幕大赛的三项任务来挑战人工智能模型：将笑话与卡通相匹配、识别获胜字幕以及解释获胜字幕为何有趣。这些任务逐渐概括了“理解”卡通的更复杂的方面；关键要素是图像和说明之间复杂且常常令人惊讶的关系，以及经常包含对人类经验和文化的间接和有趣的暗示。我们研究了多模态模型和纯语言模型：前者直接接受卡通图像的挑战，而后者则给出视觉场景的多方面描述以模拟人类水平的视觉理解。我们发现两种类型的模型都难以完成所有三项任务。例如，我们最好的多模态模型在匹配任务上的准确度落后于人类表现 30 个点，而且，即使提供了真实的视觉场景描述符，在超过 2/3 的情况下，人类编写的解释也比最好的机器编写的解释（少样本 GPT-4）更受欢迎。我们发布模型、代码、排行榜和语料库，其中包括新收集的注释，描述图像的位置/实体、场景中的异常情况以及笑话的解释。

AI.tldr: 本论文通过三个任务评估AI模型对幽默的理解能力，利用《纽约客》漫画配文比赛数据，测试模型在匹配笑话、识别获奖配文及解释笑点方面的表现。
AI.motivation: 尽管大型神经网络能生成笑话，但其是否真正理解幽默仍不清楚。本研究旨在建立基准测试，挑战AI模型在复杂幽默理解任务中的表现，探索其与人类理解的差距。
AI.method: 研究采用多模态和语言模型，前者直接处理漫画图像，后者使用多方面视觉描述模拟人类理解，通过三个任务评估模型性能。
AI.result: 实验发现两种模型在所有任务中均表现欠佳，最佳多模态模型在匹配任务上落后人类30个百分点，且人类解释在多数情况下优于机器生成的解释。
AI.conclusion: 结论表明当前AI模型在理解复杂、文化相关的幽默方面存在显著差距，需进一步研究以提升模型的幽默理解能力。

search_text: Do Androids Laugh at Electric Sheep? Humor "Understanding" Benchmarks from The New Yorker Caption Contest 机器人会嘲笑电子羊吗？ 《纽约客》字幕大赛的幽默“理解”基准 Large neural networks can now generate jokes, but do they really "understand" humor? We challenge AI models with three tasks derived from the New Yorker Cartoon Caption Contest: matching a joke to a cartoon, identifying a winning caption, and explaining why a winning caption is funny. These tasks encapsulate progressively more sophisticated aspects of "understanding" a cartoon; key elements are the complex, often surprising relationships between images and captions and the frequent inclusion of indirect and playful allusions to human experience and culture. We investigate both multimodal and language-only models: the former are challenged with the cartoon images directly, while the latter are given multifaceted descriptions of the visual scene to simulate human-level visual understanding. We find that both types of models struggle at all three tasks. For example, our best multimodal models fall 30 accuracy points behind human performance on the matching task, and, even when provided ground-truth visual scene descriptors, human-authored explanations are preferred head-to-head over the best machine-authored ones (few-shot GPT-4) in more than 2/3 of cases. We release models, code, leaderboard, and corpus, which includes newly-gathered annotations describing the image's locations/entities, what's unusual in the scene, and an explanation of the joke. 大型神经网络现在可以生成笑话，但它们真的“理解”幽默吗？我们用源自《纽约客》卡通字幕大赛的三项任务来挑战人工智能模型：将笑话与卡通相匹配、识别获胜字幕以及解释获胜字幕为何有趣。这些任务逐渐概括了“理解”卡通的更复杂的方面；关键要素是图像和说明之间复杂且常常令人惊讶的关系，以及经常包含对人类经验和文化的间接和有趣的暗示。我们研究了多模态模型和纯语言模型：前者直接接受卡通图像的挑战，而后者则给出视觉场景的多方面描述以模拟人类水平的视觉理解。我们发现两种类型的模型都难以完成所有三项任务。例如，我们最好的多模态模型在匹配任务上的准确度落后于人类表现 30 个点，而且，即使提供了真实的视觉场景描述符，在超过 2/3 的情况下，人类编写的解释也比最好的机器编写的解释（少样本 GPT-4）更受欢迎。我们发布模型、代码、排行榜和语料库，其中包括新收集的注释，描述图像的位置/实体、场景中的异常情况以及笑话的解释。 本论文通过三个任务评估AI模型对幽默的理解能力，利用《纽约客》漫画配文比赛数据，测试模型在匹配笑话、识别获奖配文及解释笑点方面的表现。 Jack Hessel,Ana Marasović,Jena D. Hwang,Lillian Lee,Jeff Da,Rowan Zellers,Robert Mankoff,Yejin Choi cs.CL,cs.CV

authors_text: David Watkins,Peter Allen,Krzysztof Choromanski,Jacob Varley,Nicholas Waytowich
title: Multiple View Performers for Shape Completion
title_zh: 用于形状完成的多视图表演者
comment: 6 pages, 2 pages of references, 6 figures, 3 tables
comments: 6 pages, 2 pages of references, 6 figures, 3 tables
abstract: We propose the Multiple View Performer (MVP) - a new architecture for 3D shape completion from a series of temporally sequential views. MVP accomplishes this task by using linear-attention Transformers called Performers. Our model allows the current observation of the scene to attend to the previous ones for more accurate infilling. The history of past observations is compressed via the compact associative memory approximating modern continuous Hopfield memory, but crucially of size independent from the history length. We compare our model with several baselines for shape completion over time, demonstrating the generalization gains that MVP provides. To the best of our knowledge, MVP is the first multiple view voxel reconstruction method that does not require registration of multiple depth views and the first causal Transformer based model for 3D shape completion.
summary: We propose the Multiple View Performer (MVP) - a new architecture for 3D shape completion from a series of temporally sequential views. MVP accomplishes this task by using linear-attention Transformers called Performers. Our model allows the current observation of the scene to attend to the previous ones for more accurate infilling. The history of past observations is compressed via the compact associative memory approximating modern continuous Hopfield memory, but crucially of size independent from the history length. We compare our model with several baselines for shape completion over time, demonstrating the generalization gains that MVP provides. To the best of our knowledge, MVP is the first multiple view voxel reconstruction method that does not require registration of multiple depth views and the first causal Transformer based model for 3D shape completion.
abstract_zh: 我们提出了多视图执行器 (MVP) - 一种用于从一系列时间顺序视图完成 3D 形状的新架构。 MVP 通过使用称为 Performers 的线性注意力 Transformer 来完成此任务。我们的模型允许当前对场景的观察关注之前的场景，以实现更准确的填充。过去观察的历史通过近似现代连续霍普菲尔德记忆的紧凑联想记忆进行压缩，但至关重要的是其大小独立于历史长度。我们将我们的模型与随着时间推移形状完成情况的几个基线进行比较，展示 MVP 提供的泛化增益。据我们所知，MVP 是第一个不需要注册多个深度视图的多视图体素重建方法，也是第一个基于因果 Transformer 的 3D 形状完成模型。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为Multiple View Performer (MVP)的新架构，用于通过时间序列的多视角视图完成3D形状补全。
AI.motivation: 现有方法在处理多视角3D形状补全时存在注册复杂、无法有效利用时间序列信息等限制，导致补全精度不足。
AI.method: 采用线性注意力Transformer（Performer）架构，通过紧凑关联记忆压缩历史观测信息，使当前视角可关注历史视角实现更精确的补全，且无需深度视图注册。
AI.result: 在多个基准测试中验证了MVP的泛化优势，证明其为首个无需深度视图注册的多视角体素重建方法，也是首个基于因果Transformer的3D形状补全模型。
AI.conclusion: MVP通过时间序列建模和高效记忆机制实现了无需注册的3D补全，验证了因果Transformer在该任务中的有效性，为后续研究提供了新方向。

authors_text: Vishwesh Nath,Dong Yang,Holger R. Roth,Daguang Xu
title: Warm Start Active Learning with Proxy Labels \& Selection via Semi-Supervised Fine-Tuning
title_zh: 使用代理标签热启动主动学习并通过半监督微调进行选择
comment: 12 pages, 5 figures
comments: 12 pages, 5 figures
abstract: Which volume to annotate next is a challenging problem in building medical imaging datasets for deep learning. One of the promising methods to approach this question is active learning (AL). However, AL has been a hard nut to crack in terms of which AL algorithm and acquisition functions are most useful for which datasets. Also, the problem is exacerbated with which volumes to label first when there is zero labeled data to start with. This is known as the cold start problem in AL. We propose two novel strategies for AL specifically for 3D image segmentation. First, we tackle the cold start problem by proposing a proxy task and then utilizing uncertainty generated from the proxy task to rank the unlabeled data to be annotated. Second, we craft a two-stage learning framework for each active iteration where the unlabeled data is also used in the second stage as a semi-supervised fine-tuning strategy. We show the promise of our approach on two well-known large public datasets from medical segmentation decathlon. The results indicate that the initial selection of data and semi-supervised framework both showed significant improvement for several AL strategies.
summary: Which volume to annotate next is a challenging problem in building medical imaging datasets for deep learning. One of the promising methods to approach this question is active learning (AL). However, AL has been a hard nut to crack in terms of which AL algorithm and acquisition functions are most useful for which datasets. Also, the problem is exacerbated with which volumes to label first when there is zero labeled data to start with. This is known as the cold start problem in AL. We propose two novel strategies for AL specifically for 3D image segmentation. First, we tackle the cold start problem by proposing a proxy task and then utilizing uncertainty generated from the proxy task to rank the unlabeled data to be annotated. Second, we craft a two-stage learning framework for each active iteration where the unlabeled data is also used in the second stage as a semi-supervised fine-tuning strategy. We show the promise of our approach on two well-known large public datasets from medical segmentation decathlon. The results indicate that the initial selection of data and semi-supervised framework both showed significant improvement for several AL strategies.
abstract_zh: 在构建用于深度学习的医学成像数据集时，接下来要注释哪一卷是一个具有挑战性的问题。解决这个问题的一种有前途的方法是主动学习（AL）。然而，关于哪种 AL 算法和采集函数对哪些数据集最有用，AL 一直是一个难题。此外，当开始时标记数据为零时，首先标记哪些卷的问题会更加严重。这在 AL 中称为冷启动问题。我们提出了两种专门针对 3D 图像分割的 AL 新颖策略。首先，我们通过提出代理任务来解决冷启动问题，然后利用代理任务生成的不确定性对要注释的未标记数据进行排序。其次，我们为每个主动迭代构建了一个两阶段学习框架，其中未标记数据也在第二阶段用作半监督微调策略。我们在医学分割十项全能的两个著名的大型公共数据集上展示了我们的方法的前景。结果表明，数据和半监督框架的初始选择都显示出几种 AL 策略的显着改进。

AI.tldr: 本文针对医学图像分割中主动学习的冷启动问题，提出基于代理标签和半监督微调的两阶段策略，以优化标注数据选择。
AI.motivation: 医学影像数据集构建中，主动学习面临算法选择困难及初始标注数据缺失（冷启动）的挑战，现有方法难以有效提升标注效率。
AI.method: 提出代理任务生成不确定性指标解决冷启动，并设计两阶段学习框架：第一阶段基于代理标签筛选数据，第二阶段通过半监督微调优化模型。
AI.result: 在医学分割Decathlon数据集上验证，所提方法显著提升了多种主动学习策略的初始标注质量与模型性能。
AI.conclusion: 代理标签与半监督框架结合有效缓解冷启动问题，为医学影像主动学习提供了可行方案，未来可探索更高效的不确定性度量方式。

search_text: Warm Start Active Learning with Proxy Labels \& Selection via Semi-Supervised Fine-Tuning 使用代理标签热启动主动学习并通过半监督微调进行选择 Which volume to annotate next is a challenging problem in building medical imaging datasets for deep learning. One of the promising methods to approach this question is active learning (AL). However, AL has been a hard nut to crack in terms of which AL algorithm and acquisition functions are most useful for which datasets. Also, the problem is exacerbated with which volumes to label first when there is zero labeled data to start with. This is known as the cold start problem in AL. We propose two novel strategies for AL specifically for 3D image segmentation. First, we tackle the cold start problem by proposing a proxy task and then utilizing uncertainty generated from the proxy task to rank the unlabeled data to be annotated. Second, we craft a two-stage learning framework for each active iteration where the unlabeled data is also used in the second stage as a semi-supervised fine-tuning strategy. We show the promise of our approach on two well-known large public datasets from medical segmentation decathlon. The results indicate that the initial selection of data and semi-supervised framework both showed significant improvement for several AL strategies. 在构建用于深度学习的医学成像数据集时，接下来要注释哪一卷是一个具有挑战性的问题。解决这个问题的一种有前途的方法是主动学习（AL）。然而，关于哪种 AL 算法和采集函数对哪些数据集最有用，AL 一直是一个难题。此外，当开始时标记数据为零时，首先标记哪些卷的问题会更加严重。这在 AL 中称为冷启动问题。我们提出了两种专门针对 3D 图像分割的 AL 新颖策略。首先，我们通过提出代理任务来解决冷启动问题，然后利用代理任务生成的不确定性对要注释的未标记数据进行排序。其次，我们为每个主动迭代构建了一个两阶段学习框架，其中未标记数据也在第二阶段用作半监督微调策略。我们在医学分割十项全能的两个著名的大型公共数据集上展示了我们的方法的前景。结果表明，数据和半监督框架的初始选择都显示出几种 AL 策略的显着改进。 本文针对医学图像分割中主动学习的冷启动问题，提出基于代理标签和半监督微调的两阶段策略，以优化标注数据选择。 Vishwesh Nath,Dong Yang,Holger R. Roth,Daguang Xu cs.CV

authors_text: Sedrick Scott Keh,Steven Y. Feng,Varun Gangal,Malihe Alikhani,Eduard Hovy
title: PANCETTA: Phoneme Aware Neural Completion to Elicit Tongue Twisters Automatically
title_zh: PANCETTA：音素感知神经补全可自动引发绕口令
comment: EACL 2023. Code at https://github.com/sedrickkeh/PANCETTA
comments: EACL 2023. Code at https://github.com/sedrickkeh/PANCETTA
abstract: Tongue twisters are meaningful sentences that are difficult to pronounce. The process of automatically generating tongue twisters is challenging since the generated utterance must satisfy two conditions at once: phonetic difficulty and semantic meaning. Furthermore, phonetic difficulty is itself hard to characterize and is expressed in natural tongue twisters through a heterogeneous mix of phenomena such as alliteration and homophony. In this paper, we propose PANCETTA: Phoneme Aware Neural Completion to Elicit Tongue Twisters Automatically. We leverage phoneme representations to capture the notion of phonetic difficulty, and we train language models to generate original tongue twisters on two proposed task settings. To do this, we curate a dataset called PANCETTA, consisting of existing English tongue twisters. Through automatic and human evaluation, as well as qualitative analysis, we show that PANCETTA generates novel, phonetically difficult, fluent, and semantically meaningful tongue twisters.
summary: Tongue twisters are meaningful sentences that are difficult to pronounce. The process of automatically generating tongue twisters is challenging since the generated utterance must satisfy two conditions at once: phonetic difficulty and semantic meaning. Furthermore, phonetic difficulty is itself hard to characterize and is expressed in natural tongue twisters through a heterogeneous mix of phenomena such as alliteration and homophony. In this paper, we propose PANCETTA: Phoneme Aware Neural Completion to Elicit Tongue Twisters Automatically. We leverage phoneme representations to capture the notion of phonetic difficulty, and we train language models to generate original tongue twisters on two proposed task settings. To do this, we curate a dataset called PANCETTA, consisting of existing English tongue twisters. Through automatic and human evaluation, as well as qualitative analysis, we show that PANCETTA generates novel, phonetically difficult, fluent, and semantically meaningful tongue twisters.
abstract_zh: 绕口令是一些有意义但很难发音的句子。自动生成绕口令的过程具有挑战性，因为生成的话语必须同时满足两个条件：语音难度和语义。此外，语音困难本身很难表征，并且通过头韵和同音异义等现象的异质组合以自然绕口令的形式表达。在本文中，我们提出了 PANCETTA：音素感知神经补全以自动引发绕口令。我们利用音素表示来捕获语音难度的概念，并训练语言模型以在两个建议的任务设置上生成原始绕口令。为此，我们策划了一个名为 PANCETTA 的数据集，其中包含现有的英语绕口令。通过自动和人工评估以及定性分析，我们表明 PANCETTA 生成新颖、语音困难、流畅且语义有意义的绕口令。

AI.tldr: 本文提出PANCETTA方法，通过音素感知的神经网络模型自动生成符合语义且具有发音难度的绕口令。
AI.motivation: 绕口令需同时满足发音难度和语义合理性，但现有方法难以有效表征发音难度的多维度特征（如头韵、同音异形等）。
AI.method: 构建包含现有英语绕口令的PANCETTA数据集，利用音素表示捕捉发音难度，并训练语言模型在两种任务设置下生成绕口令。
AI.result: 实验表明PANCETTA生成的绕口令在新颖性、发音难度、流畅度和语义合理性方面均表现优异，通过自动评估和人工评估验证。
AI.conclusion: PANCETTA通过音素感知建模有效解决了发音难度与语义意义的双重挑战，为自动绕口令生成提供了新方法和数据集。

authors_text: Daniel Korzekwa
title: Automated detection of pronunciation errors in non-native English speech employing deep learning
title_zh: 利用深度学习自动检测非母语英语语音中的发音错误
comment: PhD Thesis, in English + extended summary in Polish
comments: PhD Thesis, in English + extended summary in Polish
abstract: Despite significant advances in recent years, the existing Computer-Assisted Pronunciation Training (CAPT) methods detect pronunciation errors with a relatively low accuracy (precision of 60% at 40%-80% recall). This Ph.D. work proposes novel deep learning methods for detecting pronunciation errors in non-native (L2) English speech, outperforming the state-of-the-art method in AUC metric (Area under the Curve) by 41%, i.e., from 0.528 to 0.749. One of the problems with existing CAPT methods is the low availability of annotated mispronounced speech needed for reliable training of pronunciation error detection models. Therefore, the detection of pronunciation errors is reformulated to the task of generating synthetic mispronounced speech. Intuitively, if we could mimic mispronounced speech and produce any amount of training data, detecting pronunciation errors would be more effective. Furthermore, to eliminate the need to align canonical and recognized phonemes, a novel end-to-end multi-task technique to directly detect pronunciation errors was proposed. The pronunciation error detection models have been used at Amazon to automatically detect pronunciation errors in synthetic speech to accelerate the research into new speech synthesis methods. It was demonstrated that the proposed deep learning methods are applicable in the tasks of detecting and reconstructing dysarthric speech.
summary: Despite significant advances in recent years, the existing Computer-Assisted Pronunciation Training (CAPT) methods detect pronunciation errors with a relatively low accuracy (precision of 60% at 40%-80% recall). This Ph.D. work proposes novel deep learning methods for detecting pronunciation errors in non-native (L2) English speech, outperforming the state-of-the-art method in AUC metric (Area under the Curve) by 41%, i.e., from 0.528 to 0.749. One of the problems with existing CAPT methods is the low availability of annotated mispronounced speech needed for reliable training of pronunciation error detection models. Therefore, the detection of pronunciation errors is reformulated to the task of generating synthetic mispronounced speech. Intuitively, if we could mimic mispronounced speech and produce any amount of training data, detecting pronunciation errors would be more effective. Furthermore, to eliminate the need to align canonical and recognized phonemes, a novel end-to-end multi-task technique to directly detect pronunciation errors was proposed. The pronunciation error detection models have been used at Amazon to automatically detect pronunciation errors in synthetic speech to accelerate the research into new speech synthesis methods. It was demonstrated that the proposed deep learning methods are applicable in the tasks of detecting and reconstructing dysarthric speech.
abstract_zh: 尽管近年来取得了重大进展，但现有的计算机辅助发音训练 (CAPT) 方法检测发音错误的准确度相对较低（在 40%-80% 的召回率下，准确度为 60%）。这个博士。这项工作提出了一种新颖的深度学习方法，用于检测非母语 (L2) 英语语音中的发音错误，其 AUC 指标（曲线下面积）优于最先进的方法 41%，即从 0.528 到 0.749。现有 CAPT 方法的问题之一是可靠训练发音错误检测模型所需的注释错误语音的可用性较低。因此，发音错误的检测被重新表述为生成合成发音错误的语音的任务。直观上，如果我们能够模仿发音错误的语音并产生任意数量的训练数据，那么检测发音错误将会更加有效。此外，为了消除对齐规范和识别音素的需要，提出了一种直接检测发音错误的新颖的端到端多任务技术。发音错误检测模型已在亚马逊用于自动检测合成语音中的发音错误，以加速新语音合成方法的研究。事实证明，所提出的深度学习方法适用于检测和重建构音障碍语音的任务。

AI.tldr: 本论文旨在利用深度学习技术提升非母语者英语发音错误的自动检测准确率。
AI.motivation: 现有CAPT方法因标注数据不足和检测准确率低（精度60%、召回率40%-80%），需改进方法以提高检测效果并减少对标注数据的依赖。
AI.method: 提出生成合成错误发音语音以解决数据不足问题，并设计端到端多任务模型直接检测发音错误，无需音素对齐。
AI.result: 模型AUC指标提升41%（0.528→0.749），并在亚马逊合成语音检测及失语症语音处理中验证有效性。
AI.conclusion: 深度学习方法显著提升发音错误检测性能，且适用于合成语音与失语症语音处理，具有实际应用价值。

authors_text: Mikhail Sokolov,Christopher Henry,Joni Storie,Christopher Storie,Victor Alhassan,Mathieu Turgeon-Pelchat
title: High-resolution semantically-consistent image-to-image translation
title_zh: 高分辨率语义一致的图像到图像的翻译
comment: 25 pages, 7 figures
comments: 25 pages, 7 figures
abstract: Deep learning has become one of remote sensing scientists' most efficient computer vision tools in recent years. However, the lack of training labels for the remote sensing datasets means that scientists need to solve the domain adaptation problem to narrow the discrepancy between satellite image datasets. As a result, image segmentation models that are then trained, could better generalize and use an existing set of labels instead of requiring new ones. This work proposes an unsupervised domain adaptation model that preserves semantic consistency and per-pixel quality for the images during the style-transferring phase. This paper's major contribution is proposing the improved architecture of the SemI2I model, which significantly boosts the proposed model's performance and makes it competitive with the state-of-the-art CyCADA model. A second contribution is testing the CyCADA model on the remote sensing multi-band datasets such as WorldView-2 and SPOT-6. The proposed model preserves semantic consistency and per-pixel quality for the images during the style-transferring phase. Thus, the semantic segmentation model, trained on the adapted images, shows substantial performance gain compared to the SemI2I model and reaches similar results as the state-of-the-art CyCADA model. The future development of the proposed method could include ecological domain transfer, {\em a priori} evaluation of dataset quality in terms of data distribution, or exploration of the inner architecture of the domain adaptation model.
summary: Deep learning has become one of remote sensing scientists' most efficient computer vision tools in recent years. However, the lack of training labels for the remote sensing datasets means that scientists need to solve the domain adaptation problem to narrow the discrepancy between satellite image datasets. As a result, image segmentation models that are then trained, could better generalize and use an existing set of labels instead of requiring new ones. This work proposes an unsupervised domain adaptation model that preserves semantic consistency and per-pixel quality for the images during the style-transferring phase. This paper's major contribution is proposing the improved architecture of the SemI2I model, which significantly boosts the proposed model's performance and makes it competitive with the state-of-the-art CyCADA model. A second contribution is testing the CyCADA model on the remote sensing multi-band datasets such as WorldView-2 and SPOT-6. The proposed model preserves semantic consistency and per-pixel quality for the images during the style-transferring phase. Thus, the semantic segmentation model, trained on the adapted images, shows substantial performance gain compared to the SemI2I model and reaches similar results as the state-of-the-art CyCADA model. The future development of the proposed method could include ecological domain transfer, {\em a priori} evaluation of dataset quality in terms of data distribution, or exploration of the inner architecture of the domain adaptation model.
abstract_zh: 近年来，深度学习已成为遥感科学家最高效的计算机视觉工具之一。然而，遥感数据集缺乏训练标签意味着科学家需要解决域适应问题以缩小卫星图像数据集之间的差异。因此，经过训练的图像分割模型可以更好地概括和使用现有的标签集，而不需要新的标签。这项工作提出了一种无监督的域适应模型，可以在风格转移阶段保持图像的语义一致性和每像素质量。本文的主要贡献是提出了 SemI2I 模型的改进架构，这显着提高了所提出模型的性能，并使其与最先进的 CyCADA 模型具有竞争力。第二个贡献是在 WorldView-2 和 SPOT-6 等遥感多波段数据集上测试 CyCADA 模型。所提出的模型在风格转移阶段保留了图像的语义一致性和每像素质量。因此，在适应图像上训练的语义分割模型与 SemI2I 模型相比显示出显着的性能增益，并达到与最先进的 CyCADA 模型相似的结果。该方法的未来发展可能包括生态域转移、在数据分布方面对数据集质量进行先验评估或探索域适应模型的内部架构。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种无监督域适应模型，用于在遥感图像中保持语义一致性和像素质量的图像到图像转换。
AI.motivation: 遥感数据集缺乏训练标签，科学家需解决域适应问题以缩小卫星图像数据集间的差异，从而提升分割模型的泛化能力。
AI.method: 改进SemI2I模型架构，提出保留语义一致性和像素质量的无监督域适应方法，并在WorldView-2和SPOT-6等多光谱数据集上测试CyCADA模型。
AI.result: 所提模型在语义分割任务中显著优于SemI2I模型，性能接近最先进的CyCADA模型。
AI.conclusion: 未来可探索生态域迁移、数据分布质量评估及域适应模型内部结构优化等方向。

authors_text: Sridhar Mahadevan
title: Unifying Causal Inference and Reinforcement Learning using Higher-Order Category Theory
title_zh: 使用高阶类别理论统一因果推理和强化学习
comment: 21 pages
comments: 21 pages
abstract: We present a unified formalism for structure discovery of causal models and predictive state representation (PSR) models in reinforcement learning (RL) using higher-order category theory. Specifically, we model structure discovery in both settings using simplicial objects, contravariant functors from the category of ordinal numbers into any category. Fragments of causal models that are equivalent under conditional independence -- defined as causal horns -- as well as subsequences of potential tests in a predictive state representation -- defined as predictive horns -- are both special cases of horns of a simplicial object, subsets resulting from the removal of the interior and the face opposite a particular vertex. Latent structure discovery in both settings involve the same fundamental mathematical problem of finding extensions of horns of simplicial objects through solving lifting problems in commutative diagrams, and exploiting weak homotopies that define higher-order symmetries. Solutions to the problem of filling "inner" vs "outer" horns leads to various notions of higher-order categories, including weak Kan complexes and quasicategories. We define the abstract problem of structure discovery in both settings in terms of adjoint functors between the category of universal causal models or universal decision models and its simplicial object representation.
summary: We present a unified formalism for structure discovery of causal models and predictive state representation (PSR) models in reinforcement learning (RL) using higher-order category theory. Specifically, we model structure discovery in both settings using simplicial objects, contravariant functors from the category of ordinal numbers into any category. Fragments of causal models that are equivalent under conditional independence -- defined as causal horns -- as well as subsequences of potential tests in a predictive state representation -- defined as predictive horns -- are both special cases of horns of a simplicial object, subsets resulting from the removal of the interior and the face opposite a particular vertex. Latent structure discovery in both settings involve the same fundamental mathematical problem of finding extensions of horns of simplicial objects through solving lifting problems in commutative diagrams, and exploiting weak homotopies that define higher-order symmetries. Solutions to the problem of filling "inner" vs "outer" horns leads to various notions of higher-order categories, including weak Kan complexes and quasicategories. We define the abstract problem of structure discovery in both settings in terms of adjoint functors between the category of universal causal models or universal decision models and its simplicial object representation.
abstract_zh: 我们使用高阶类别理论提出了强化学习（RL）中因果模型和预测状态表示（PSR）模型的结构发现的统一形式。具体来说，我们使用单纯对象、从序数类别到任何类别的逆变函子来对这两种设置中的结构发现进行建模。在条件独立下等效的因果模型片段（定义为因果角）以及预测状态表示中的潜在测试的子序列（定义为预测角）都是单纯对象角的特殊情况，是由于删除内部和与特定顶点相对的面而产生的子集。这两种设置中的潜在结构发现都涉及相同的基本数学问题，即通过解决交换图中的提升问题来找到单纯对象角的扩展，并利用定义高阶对称性的弱同伦。填充“内部”角与“外部”角问题的解决方案导致了各种高阶类别的概念，包括弱 Kan 复合体和准类别。我们根据通用因果模型或通用决策模型及其单纯对象表示之间的伴随函子来定义这两种设置中的结构发现的抽象问题。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于高阶范畴论的统一形式化方法，用于强化学习中因果模型和预测状态表示模型的结构发现。
AI.motivation: 因果推理与强化学习在结构发现任务中存在共性问题，但传统方法缺乏统一的数学框架，限制了跨领域方法的迁移与融合。
AI.method: 通过将因果模型片段（因果角）和预测状态子序列（预测角）建模为单纯形对象的子集，利用范畴论中的反变函子、提升问题求解及弱同伦理论构建统一数学框架。
AI.result: 揭示了因果推理与强化学习结构发现的本质同源性，建立了基于单纯形对象扩展的高阶范畴理论体系，包括弱Kan复体等结构。
AI.conclusion: 该工作为因果推理与强化学习提供了抽象统一的数学基础，可能推动两领域方法论的深度融合与创新。
