authors_text: Kun Wang,William R. Johnson III,Shiyang Lu,Xiaonan Huang,Joran Booth,Rebecca Kramer-Bottiglio,Mridul Aanjaneya,Kostas Bekris
title: Real2Sim2Real Transfer for Control of Cable-driven Robots via a Differentiable Physics Engine
title_zh: 通过可微分物理引擎控制电缆驱动机器人的 Real2Sim2Real 传输
comment: Accepted to IROS2023; https://sites.google.com/view/sim2real
comments: Accepted to IROS2023; https://sites.google.com/view/sim2real
abstract: Tensegrity robots, composed of rigid rods and flexible cables, exhibit high strength-to-weight ratios and significant deformations, which enable them to navigate unstructured terrains and survive harsh impacts. They are hard to control, however, due to high dimensionality, complex dynamics, and a coupled architecture. Physics-based simulation is a promising avenue for developing locomotion policies that can be transferred to real robots. Nevertheless, modeling tensegrity robots is a complex task due to a substantial sim2real gap. To address this issue, this paper describes a Real2Sim2Real (R2S2R) strategy for tensegrity robots. This strategy is based on a differentiable physics engine that can be trained given limited data from a real robot. These data include offline measurements of physical properties, such as mass and geometry for various robot components, and the observation of a trajectory using a random control policy. With the data from the real robot, the engine can be iteratively refined and used to discover locomotion policies that are directly transferable to the real robot. Beyond the R2S2R pipeline, key contributions of this work include computing non-zero gradients at contact points, a loss function for matching tensegrity locomotion gaits, and a trajectory segmentation technique that avoids conflicts in gradient evaluation during training. Multiple iterations of the R2S2R process are demonstrated and evaluated on a real 3-bar tensegrity robot.
summary: Tensegrity robots, composed of rigid rods and flexible cables, exhibit high strength-to-weight ratios and significant deformations, which enable them to navigate unstructured terrains and survive harsh impacts. They are hard to control, however, due to high dimensionality, complex dynamics, and a coupled architecture. Physics-based simulation is a promising avenue for developing locomotion policies that can be transferred to real robots. Nevertheless, modeling tensegrity robots is a complex task due to a substantial sim2real gap. To address this issue, this paper describes a Real2Sim2Real (R2S2R) strategy for tensegrity robots. This strategy is based on a differentiable physics engine that can be trained given limited data from a real robot. These data include offline measurements of physical properties, such as mass and geometry for various robot components, and the observation of a trajectory using a random control policy. With the data from the real robot, the engine can be iteratively refined and used to discover locomotion policies that are directly transferable to the real robot. Beyond the R2S2R pipeline, key contributions of this work include computing non-zero gradients at contact points, a loss function for matching tensegrity locomotion gaits, and a trajectory segmentation technique that avoids conflicts in gradient evaluation during training. Multiple iterations of the R2S2R process are demonstrated and evaluated on a real 3-bar tensegrity robot.
abstract_zh: 张拉整体机器人由刚性杆和柔性电缆组成，具有高强度重量比和显着的变形，这使它们能够在非结构化地形中行驶并承受严酷的冲击。然而，由于高维度、复杂的动力学和耦合架构，它们很难控制。基于物理的模拟是开发可转移到真实机器人的运动策略的一个有前途的途径。然而，由于 sim2real 之间存在巨大差距，张拉整体机器人建模是一项复杂的任务。为了解决这个问题，本文描述了张拉整体机器人的 Real2Sim2Real (R2S2R) 策略。该策略基于可微的物理引擎，可以在来自真实机器人的有限数据的情况下进行训练。这些数据包括物理属性的离线测量，例如各种机器人组件的质量和几何形状，以及使用随机控制策略对轨迹的观察。利用来自真实机器人的数据，引擎可以迭代地完善并用于发现可直接转移到真实机器人的运动策略。除了 R2S2R 管道之外，这项工作的主要贡献包括计算接触点处的非零梯度、用于匹配张拉整体运动步态的损失函数，以及避免训练期间梯度评估冲突的轨迹分割技术。 R2S2R 过程的多次迭代在真实的 3 杆张拉整体机器人上进行了演示和评估。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于可微分物理引擎的Real2Sim2Real（R2S2R）策略，用于解决张力结构机器人的控制问题，通过有限的真实机器人数据迭代优化仿真模型，实现控制策略的跨仿真-现实转移。
AI.motivation: 张力结构机器人因高自由度和复杂动力学难以直接控制，传统物理仿真存在显著的仿真到现实（sim2real）偏差，需开发新的方法缩小这一差距以实现有效控制。
AI.method: 构建可微分物理引擎，利用真实机器人离线测量的物理参数（质量、几何）和随机控制策略下的轨迹数据，迭代优化仿真模型；提出接触点非零梯度计算、步态匹配损失函数及轨迹分割技术以提升训练效率。
AI.result: 在真实三杆张力结构机器人上验证了R2S2R流程的多轮迭代效果，成功生成可直接迁移至实物的运动控制策略，显著缩小了仿真与现实的性能差距。
AI.conclusion: R2S2R框架通过数据驱动的仿真优化有效解决了张力结构机器人的控制难题，为有限数据场景下的物理仿真与现实系统协同提供了新方法。

search_text: Real2Sim2Real Transfer for Control of Cable-driven Robots via a Differentiable Physics Engine 通过可微分物理引擎控制电缆驱动机器人的 Real2Sim2Real 传输 Tensegrity robots, composed of rigid rods and flexible cables, exhibit high strength-to-weight ratios and significant deformations, which enable them to navigate unstructured terrains and survive harsh impacts. They are hard to control, however, due to high dimensionality, complex dynamics, and a coupled architecture. Physics-based simulation is a promising avenue for developing locomotion policies that can be transferred to real robots. Nevertheless, modeling tensegrity robots is a complex task due to a substantial sim2real gap. To address this issue, this paper describes a Real2Sim2Real (R2S2R) strategy for tensegrity robots. This strategy is based on a differentiable physics engine that can be trained given limited data from a real robot. These data include offline measurements of physical properties, such as mass and geometry for various robot components, and the observation of a trajectory using a random control policy. With the data from the real robot, the engine can be iteratively refined and used to discover locomotion policies that are directly transferable to the real robot. Beyond the R2S2R pipeline, key contributions of this work include computing non-zero gradients at contact points, a loss function for matching tensegrity locomotion gaits, and a trajectory segmentation technique that avoids conflicts in gradient evaluation during training. Multiple iterations of the R2S2R process are demonstrated and evaluated on a real 3-bar tensegrity robot. 张拉整体机器人由刚性杆和柔性电缆组成，具有高强度重量比和显着的变形，这使它们能够在非结构化地形中行驶并承受严酷的冲击。然而，由于高维度、复杂的动力学和耦合架构，它们很难控制。基于物理的模拟是开发可转移到真实机器人的运动策略的一个有前途的途径。然而，由于 sim2real 之间存在巨大差距，张拉整体机器人建模是一项复杂的任务。为了解决这个问题，本文描述了张拉整体机器人的 Real2Sim2Real (R2S2R) 策略。该策略基于可微的物理引擎，可以在来自真实机器人的有限数据的情况下进行训练。这些数据包括物理属性的离线测量，例如各种机器人组件的质量和几何形状，以及使用随机控制策略对轨迹的观察。利用来自真实机器人的数据，引擎可以迭代地完善并用于发现可直接转移到真实机器人的运动策略。除了 R2S2R 管道之外，这项工作的主要贡献包括计算接触点处的非零梯度、用于匹配张拉整体运动步态的损失函数，以及避免训练期间梯度评估冲突的轨迹分割技术。 R2S2R 过程的多次迭代在真实的 3 杆张拉整体机器人上进行了演示和评估。 本文提出了一种基于可微分物理引擎的Real2Sim2Real（R2S2R）策略，用于解决张力结构机器人的控制问题，通过有限的真实机器人数据迭代优化仿真模型，实现控制策略的跨仿真-现实转移。 Kun Wang,William R. Johnson III,Shiyang Lu,Xiaonan Huang,Joran Booth,Rebecca Kramer-Bottiglio,Mridul Aanjaneya,Kostas Bekris cs.RO,cs.AI,cs.GR,cs.LG,eess.SY

authors_text: Leo Feng,Padideh Nouri,Aneri Muni,Yoshua Bengio,Pierre-Luc Bacon
title: Designing Biological Sequences via Meta-Reinforcement Learning and Bayesian Optimization
title_zh: 通过元强化学习和贝叶斯优化设计生物序列
abstract: The ability to accelerate the design of biological sequences can have a substantial impact on the progress of the medical field. The problem can be framed as a global optimization problem where the objective is an expensive black-box function such that we can query large batches restricted with a limitation of a low number of rounds. Bayesian Optimization is a principled method for tackling this problem. However, the astronomically large state space of biological sequences renders brute-force iterating over all possible sequences infeasible. In this paper, we propose MetaRLBO where we train an autoregressive generative model via Meta-Reinforcement Learning to propose promising sequences for selection via Bayesian Optimization. We pose this problem as that of finding an optimal policy over a distribution of MDPs induced by sampling subsets of the data acquired in the previous rounds. Our in-silico experiments show that meta-learning over such ensembles provides robustness against reward misspecification and achieves competitive results compared to existing strong baselines.
summary: The ability to accelerate the design of biological sequences can have a substantial impact on the progress of the medical field. The problem can be framed as a global optimization problem where the objective is an expensive black-box function such that we can query large batches restricted with a limitation of a low number of rounds. Bayesian Optimization is a principled method for tackling this problem. However, the astronomically large state space of biological sequences renders brute-force iterating over all possible sequences infeasible. In this paper, we propose MetaRLBO where we train an autoregressive generative model via Meta-Reinforcement Learning to propose promising sequences for selection via Bayesian Optimization. We pose this problem as that of finding an optimal policy over a distribution of MDPs induced by sampling subsets of the data acquired in the previous rounds. Our in-silico experiments show that meta-learning over such ensembles provides robustness against reward misspecification and achieves competitive results compared to existing strong baselines.
abstract_zh: 加速生物序列设计的能力可以对医学领域的进步产生重大影响。该问题可以被视为全局优化问题，其中目标是昂贵的黑盒函数，以便我们可以查询受低轮数限制的大批量。贝叶斯优化是解决这个问题的一种原则方法。然而，生物序列的状态空间巨大，使得对所有可能的序列进行暴力迭代是不可行的。在本文中，我们提出了 MetaRLBO，其中我们通过元强化学习训练自回归生成模型，以通过贝叶斯优化提出有希望的序列进行选择。我们提出这个问题，即通过对前几轮中获取的数据子集进行采样而得出的 MDP 分布找到最优策略。我们的计算机实验表明，与现有的强大基线相比，对此类集成的元学习提供了针对奖励错误指定的鲁棒性，并实现了有竞争力的结果。

AI.tldr: 通过元强化学习与贝叶斯优化方法加速生物序列设计
AI.motivation: 生物序列设计对医学发展至关重要，但传统方法因状态空间巨大导致效率低下，需更高效优化策略
AI.method: 提出MetaRLBO方法，结合元强化学习训练自回归生成模型与贝叶斯优化，通过元学习在多个MDP中寻找最优策略
AI.result: 实验表明方法在奖励函数不准确时更具鲁棒性，效果与现有强基线相当
AI.conclusion: 该方法有效解决生物序列设计难题，为相关领域提供新思路

authors_text: Liron Simon Keren,Alex Liberzon,Teddy Lazebnik
title: A computational framework for physics-informed symbolic regression with straightforward integration of domain knowledge
title_zh: 用于物理信息符号回归的计算框架，可直接集成领域知识
abstract: Discovering a meaningful symbolic expression that explains experimental data is a fundamental challenge in many scientific fields. We present a novel, open-source computational framework called Scientist-Machine Equation Detector (SciMED), which integrates scientific discipline wisdom in a scientist-in-the-loop approach, with state-of-the-art symbolic regression (SR) methods. SciMED combines a wrapper selection method, that is based on a genetic algorithm, with automatic machine learning and two levels of SR methods. We test SciMED on five configurations of a settling sphere, with and without aerodynamic non-linear drag force, and with excessive noise in the measurements. We show that SciMED is sufficiently robust to discover the correct physically meaningful symbolic expressions from the data, and demonstrate how the integration of domain knowledge enhances its performance. Our results indicate better performance on these tasks than the state-of-the-art SR software packages , even in cases where no knowledge is integrated. Moreover, we demonstrate how SciMED can alert the user about possible missing features, unlike the majority of current SR systems.
summary: Discovering a meaningful symbolic expression that explains experimental data is a fundamental challenge in many scientific fields. We present a novel, open-source computational framework called Scientist-Machine Equation Detector (SciMED), which integrates scientific discipline wisdom in a scientist-in-the-loop approach, with state-of-the-art symbolic regression (SR) methods. SciMED combines a wrapper selection method, that is based on a genetic algorithm, with automatic machine learning and two levels of SR methods. We test SciMED on five configurations of a settling sphere, with and without aerodynamic non-linear drag force, and with excessive noise in the measurements. We show that SciMED is sufficiently robust to discover the correct physically meaningful symbolic expressions from the data, and demonstrate how the integration of domain knowledge enhances its performance. Our results indicate better performance on these tasks than the state-of-the-art SR software packages , even in cases where no knowledge is integrated. Moreover, we demonstrate how SciMED can alert the user about possible missing features, unlike the majority of current SR systems.
abstract_zh: 发现解释实验数据的有意义的符号表达是许多科学领域的基本挑战。我们提出了一种新颖的开源计算框架，称为科学家机器方程检测器 (SciMED)，它将科学学科智慧与科学家在环方法中集成，并采用最先进的符号回归 (SR) 方法。 SciMED 将基于遗传算法的包装选择方法与自动机器学习和两级 SR 方法相结合。我们在沉降球的五种配置上测试 SciMED，有或没有空气动力非线性阻力，并且测量中噪音过大。我们证明 SciMED 足够强大，可以从数据中发现正确的有物理意义的符号表达式，并演示领域知识的集成如何增强其性能。我们的结果表明，即使在没有集成知识的情况下，这些任务的性能也比最先进的 SR 软件包更好。此外，我们还演示了 SciMED 如何提醒用户可能缺少的功能，这与当前大多数 SR 系统不同。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为SciMED的计算框架，通过整合领域知识提升符号回归在物理问题中的表现。
AI.motivation: 科学领域亟需从实验数据中发现具有物理意义的符号表达式，但现有符号回归方法在复杂场景下存在鲁棒性和可解释性不足的问题。
AI.method: 开发SciMED框架，结合遗传算法的包装选择方法、自动机器学习及两级符号回归技术，通过科学家参与的闭环流程整合领域知识。
AI.result: 在含噪声的沉降球实验数据集上，SciMED成功恢复了正确的物理公式，性能优于现有符号回归软件，且能主动提示潜在缺失特征。
AI.conclusion: SciMED通过系统化整合领域知识显著提升了符号回归的准确性与可靠性，为物理启发式机器学习提供了新范式。

search_text: A computational framework for physics-informed symbolic regression with straightforward integration of domain knowledge 用于物理信息符号回归的计算框架，可直接集成领域知识 Discovering a meaningful symbolic expression that explains experimental data is a fundamental challenge in many scientific fields. We present a novel, open-source computational framework called Scientist-Machine Equation Detector (SciMED), which integrates scientific discipline wisdom in a scientist-in-the-loop approach, with state-of-the-art symbolic regression (SR) methods. SciMED combines a wrapper selection method, that is based on a genetic algorithm, with automatic machine learning and two levels of SR methods. We test SciMED on five configurations of a settling sphere, with and without aerodynamic non-linear drag force, and with excessive noise in the measurements. We show that SciMED is sufficiently robust to discover the correct physically meaningful symbolic expressions from the data, and demonstrate how the integration of domain knowledge enhances its performance. Our results indicate better performance on these tasks than the state-of-the-art SR software packages , even in cases where no knowledge is integrated. Moreover, we demonstrate how SciMED can alert the user about possible missing features, unlike the majority of current SR systems. 发现解释实验数据的有意义的符号表达是许多科学领域的基本挑战。我们提出了一种新颖的开源计算框架，称为科学家机器方程检测器 (SciMED)，它将科学学科智慧与科学家在环方法中集成，并采用最先进的符号回归 (SR) 方法。 SciMED 将基于遗传算法的包装选择方法与自动机器学习和两级 SR 方法相结合。我们在沉降球的五种配置上测试 SciMED，有或没有空气动力非线性阻力，并且测量中噪音过大。我们证明 SciMED 足够强大，可以从数据中发现正确的有物理意义的符号表达式，并演示领域知识的集成如何增强其性能。我们的结果表明，即使在没有集成知识的情况下，这些任务的性能也比最先进的 SR 软件包更好。此外，我们还演示了 SciMED 如何提醒用户可能缺少的功能，这与当前大多数 SR 系统不同。 本文提出了一种名为SciMED的计算框架，通过整合领域知识提升符号回归在物理问题中的表现。 Liron Simon Keren,Alex Liberzon,Teddy Lazebnik cs.LG,cs.CE,cs.HC,cs.IR

authors_text: Murtuza Shahzad,Hamed Alhoori,Reva Freedman,Shaikh Abdul Rahman
title: Quantifying the Online Long-Term Interest in Research
title_zh: 量化在线研究的长期兴趣
comment: Journal of Informetrics
comments: Journal of Informetrics
abstract: Research articles are being shared in increasing numbers on multiple online platforms. Although the scholarly impact of these articles has been widely studied, the online interest determined by how long the research articles are shared online remains unclear. Being cognizant of how long a research article is mentioned online could be valuable information to the researchers. In this paper, we analyzed multiple social media platforms on which users share and/or discuss scholarly articles. We built three clusters for papers, based on the number of yearly online mentions having publication dates ranging from the year 1920 to 2016. Using the online social media metrics for each of these three clusters, we built machine learning models to predict the long-term online interest in research articles. We addressed the prediction task with two different approaches: regression and classification. For the regression approach, the Multi-Layer Perceptron model performed best, and for the classification approach, the tree-based models performed better than other models. We found that old articles are most evident in the contexts of economics and industry (i.e., patents). In contrast, recently published articles are most evident in research platforms (i.e., Mendeley) followed by social media platforms (i.e., Twitter).
summary: Research articles are being shared in increasing numbers on multiple online platforms. Although the scholarly impact of these articles has been widely studied, the online interest determined by how long the research articles are shared online remains unclear. Being cognizant of how long a research article is mentioned online could be valuable information to the researchers. In this paper, we analyzed multiple social media platforms on which users share and/or discuss scholarly articles. We built three clusters for papers, based on the number of yearly online mentions having publication dates ranging from the year 1920 to 2016. Using the online social media metrics for each of these three clusters, we built machine learning models to predict the long-term online interest in research articles. We addressed the prediction task with two different approaches: regression and classification. For the regression approach, the Multi-Layer Perceptron model performed best, and for the classification approach, the tree-based models performed better than other models. We found that old articles are most evident in the contexts of economics and industry (i.e., patents). In contrast, recently published articles are most evident in research platforms (i.e., Mendeley) followed by social media platforms (i.e., Twitter).
abstract_zh: 研究文章在多个在线平台上被分享的数量越来越多。尽管这些文章的学术影响已得到广泛研究，但由研究文章在线共享时长决定的在线兴趣仍不清楚。了解一篇研究文章在网上被提及的时间对于研究人员来说可能是有价值的信息。在本文中，我们分析了用户分享和/或讨论学术文章的多个社交媒体平台。我们根据发表日期从 1920 年到 2016 年的每年在线提及数量，为论文构建了三个集群。使用这三个集群中每个集群的在线社交媒体指标，我们构建了机器学习模型来预测研究文章的长期在线兴趣。我们使用两种不同的方法来解决预测任务：回归和分类。对于回归方法，多层感知器模型表现最好，对于分类方法，基于树的模型比其他模型表现更好。我们发现旧文章在经济和工业领域（即专利）最为明显。相比之下，最近发表的文章在研究平台（即 Mendeley）中最为明显，其次是社交媒体平台（即 Twitter）。

AI.tldr: 该论文旨在量化研究论文的在线长期兴趣度，通过分析社交媒体平台上的分享和讨论数据。
AI.motivation: 尽管学术影响力已被广泛研究，但研究论文在线上被提及的时间长短及其兴趣度变化尚未明确，这对研究人员具有重要价值。
AI.method: 通过分析多个社交媒体平台的数据，根据1920年至2016年间的年度在线提及次数将论文分为三类，并使用机器学习模型（回归和分类方法）预测长期在线兴趣。
AI.result: 回归模型中多层感知机表现最佳，分类模型中树模型效果更优。发现旧文章在经济学和工业领域（如专利）更突出，新文章在研究平台（如Mendeley）和社交媒体（如Twitter）更活跃。
AI.conclusion: 机器学习可有效预测研究论文的长期在线兴趣，不同领域和平台的传播趋势存在显著差异。

authors_text: Laércio Lima Pilla
title: Scheduling Algorithms for Federated Learning with Minimal Energy Consumption
title_zh: 最小能耗联邦学习的调度算法
abstract: Federated Learning (FL) has opened the opportunity for collaboratively training machine learning models on heterogeneous mobile or Edge devices while keeping local data private.With an increase in its adoption, a growing concern is related to its economic and environmental cost (as is also the case for other machine learning techniques).Unfortunately, little work has been done to optimize its energy consumption or emissions of carbon dioxide or equivalents, as energy minimization is usually left as a secondary objective.In this paper, we investigate the problem of minimizing the energy consumption of FL training on heterogeneous devices by controlling the workload distribution.We model this as the Minimal Cost FL Schedule problem, a total cost minimization problem with identical, independent, and atomic tasks that have to be assigned to heterogeneous resources with arbitrary cost functions.We propose a pseudo-polynomial optimal solution to the problem based on the previously unexplored Multiple-Choice Minimum-Cost Maximal Knapsack Packing Problem.We also provide four algorithms for scenarios where cost functions are monotonically increasing and follow the same behavior.These solutions are likewise applicable on the minimization of other kinds of costs, and in other one-dimensional data partition problems.
summary: Federated Learning (FL) has opened the opportunity for collaboratively training machine learning models on heterogeneous mobile or Edge devices while keeping local data private.With an increase in its adoption, a growing concern is related to its economic and environmental cost (as is also the case for other machine learning techniques).Unfortunately, little work has been done to optimize its energy consumption or emissions of carbon dioxide or equivalents, as energy minimization is usually left as a secondary objective.In this paper, we investigate the problem of minimizing the energy consumption of FL training on heterogeneous devices by controlling the workload distribution.We model this as the Minimal Cost FL Schedule problem, a total cost minimization problem with identical, independent, and atomic tasks that have to be assigned to heterogeneous resources with arbitrary cost functions.We propose a pseudo-polynomial optimal solution to the problem based on the previously unexplored Multiple-Choice Minimum-Cost Maximal Knapsack Packing Problem.We also provide four algorithms for scenarios where cost functions are monotonically increasing and follow the same behavior.These solutions are likewise applicable on the minimization of other kinds of costs, and in other one-dimensional data partition problems.
abstract_zh: 联邦学习（FL）为在异构移动或边缘设备上协作训练机器学习模型提供了机会，同时保持本地数据的私密性。随着其采用的增加，人们越来越关注其经济和环境成本（其他机器学习技术也是如此）。不幸的是，在优化其能源消耗或二氧化碳排放或同等物方面几乎没有做任何工作，因为能源最小化通常被作为次要目标。在本文中，我们研究了在异构上最小化 FL 训练的能源消耗的问题我们将其建模为最小成本 FL 调度问题，这是一个具有相同、独立和原子任务的总成本最小化问题，必须将这些任务分配给具有任意成本函数的异构资源。我们基于先前未探索的多选择最小成本最大背包打包问题，提出了该问题的伪多项式最优解决方案。我们还为成本函数单调递增并遵循相同行为的场景提供了四种算法。这些解决方案同样适用于其他类型成本的最小化，以及其他一维数据分区问题。

AI.tldr: 本文研究了在异构设备上进行联邦学习（FL）时如何通过控制工作负载分配来最小化能耗问题。
AI.motivation: 随着联邦学习的普及，其经济和环境成本（如能耗和碳排放）成为重要问题，但现有研究对能耗优化关注不足。
AI.method: 将问题建模为具有任意成本函数的异构资源任务分配问题，提出基于多选最小成本最大背包装箱问题的伪多项式最优解，并设计四种适用于单调递增成本函数的算法。
AI.result: 提出的算法可有效最小化FL训练的能耗及其他成本，适用于其他一类数据划分问题。
AI.conclusion: 本文为联邦学习的能耗优化提供了理论模型和实用算法，为降低其环境成本提供了新思路。

search_text: Scheduling Algorithms for Federated Learning with Minimal Energy Consumption 最小能耗联邦学习的调度算法 Federated Learning (FL) has opened the opportunity for collaboratively training machine learning models on heterogeneous mobile or Edge devices while keeping local data private.With an increase in its adoption, a growing concern is related to its economic and environmental cost (as is also the case for other machine learning techniques).Unfortunately, little work has been done to optimize its energy consumption or emissions of carbon dioxide or equivalents, as energy minimization is usually left as a secondary objective.In this paper, we investigate the problem of minimizing the energy consumption of FL training on heterogeneous devices by controlling the workload distribution.We model this as the Minimal Cost FL Schedule problem, a total cost minimization problem with identical, independent, and atomic tasks that have to be assigned to heterogeneous resources with arbitrary cost functions.We propose a pseudo-polynomial optimal solution to the problem based on the previously unexplored Multiple-Choice Minimum-Cost Maximal Knapsack Packing Problem.We also provide four algorithms for scenarios where cost functions are monotonically increasing and follow the same behavior.These solutions are likewise applicable on the minimization of other kinds of costs, and in other one-dimensional data partition problems. 联邦学习（FL）为在异构移动或边缘设备上协作训练机器学习模型提供了机会，同时保持本地数据的私密性。随着其采用的增加，人们越来越关注其经济和环境成本（其他机器学习技术也是如此）。不幸的是，在优化其能源消耗或二氧化碳排放或同等物方面几乎没有做任何工作，因为能源最小化通常被作为次要目标。在本文中，我们研究了在异构上最小化 FL 训练的能源消耗的问题我们将其建模为最小成本 FL 调度问题，这是一个具有相同、独立和原子任务的总成本最小化问题，必须将这些任务分配给具有任意成本函数的异构资源。我们基于先前未探索的多选择最小成本最大背包打包问题，提出了该问题的伪多项式最优解决方案。我们还为成本函数单调递增并遵循相同行为的场景提供了四种算法。这些解决方案同样适用于其他类型成本的最小化，以及其他一维数据分区问题。 本文研究了在异构设备上进行联邦学习（FL）时如何通过控制工作负载分配来最小化能耗问题。 Laércio Lima Pilla cs.LG,cs.AI,cs.DC,cs.DS

authors_text: Zijie Wang,Aichun Zhu,Jingyi Xue,Xili Wan,Chao Liu,Tian Wang,Yifeng Li
title: Look Before You Leap: Improving Text-based Person Retrieval by Learning A Consistent Cross-modal Common Manifold
title_zh: 三思而后行：通过学习一致的跨模态通用流形来改进基于文本的人物检索
comment: Accepted on ACM MM '22. arXiv admin note: text overlap with arXiv:2209.05773
comments: Accepted on ACM MM '22. arXiv admin note: text overlap with arXiv:2209.05773
abstract: The core problem of text-based person retrieval is how to bridge the heterogeneous gap between multi-modal data. Many previous approaches contrive to learning a latent common manifold mapping paradigm following a \textbf{cross-modal distribution consensus prediction (CDCP)} manner. When mapping features from distribution of one certain modality into the common manifold, feature distribution of the opposite modality is completely invisible. That is to say, how to achieve a cross-modal distribution consensus so as to embed and align the multi-modal features in a constructed cross-modal common manifold all depends on the experience of the model itself, instead of the actual situation. With such methods, it is inevitable that the multi-modal data can not be well aligned in the common manifold, which finally leads to a sub-optimal retrieval performance. To overcome this \textbf{CDCP dilemma}, we propose a novel algorithm termed LBUL to learn a Consistent Cross-modal Common Manifold (C$^{3}$M) for text-based person retrieval. The core idea of our method, just as a Chinese saying goes, is to `\textit{san si er hou xing}', namely, to \textbf{Look Before yoU Leap (LBUL)}. The common manifold mapping mechanism of LBUL contains a looking step and a leaping step. Compared to CDCP-based methods, LBUL considers distribution characteristics of both the visual and textual modalities before embedding data from one certain modality into C$^{3}$M to achieve a more solid cross-modal distribution consensus, and hence achieve a superior retrieval accuracy. We evaluate our proposed method on two text-based person retrieval datasets CUHK-PEDES and RSTPReid. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed LBUL outperforms previous methods and achieves the state-of-the-art performance.

Many previous approaches contrive to learning a latent common manifold mapping paradigm following a \textbf{cross-modal distribution consensus prediction (CDCP)} manner. When mapping features from distribution of one certain modality into the common manifold, feature distribution of the opposite modality is completely invisible. That is to say, how to achieve a cross-modal distribution consensus so as to embed and align the multi-modal features in a constructed cross-modal common manifold all depends on the experience of the model itself, instead of the actual situation. With such methods, it is inevitable that the multi-modal data can not be well aligned in the common manifold, which finally leads to a sub-optimal retrieval performance. To overcome this \textbf{CDCP dilemma}, we propose a novel algorithm termed LBUL to learn a Consistent Cross-modal Common Manifold (C$^{3}$M) for text-based person retrieval. The core idea of our method, just as a Chinese saying goes, is to `\textit{san si er hou xing}', namely, to \textbf{Look Before yoU Leap (LBUL)}. The common manifold mapping mechanism of LBUL contains a looking step and a leaping step. Compared to CDCP-based methods, LBUL considers distribution characteristics of both the visual and textual modalities before embedding data from one certain modality into C$^{3}$M to achieve a more solid cross-modal distribution consensus, and hence achieve a superior retrieval accuracy. We evaluate our proposed method on two text-based person retrieval datasets CUHK-PEDES and RSTPReid. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed LBUL outperforms previous methods and achieves the state-of-the-art performance.
abstract_zh: 基于文本的人物检索的核心问题是如何弥合多模态数据之间的异构差距。许多先前的方法致力于按照\textbf{跨模态分布共识预测（CDCP）}方式学习潜在的通用流形映射范式。当将特征从一种特定模态的分布映射到公共流形时，相反模态的特征分布是完全不可见的。也就是说，如何达成跨模态分布共识，从而将多模态特征嵌入并对齐到构建的跨模态公共流形中，这一切都取决于模型本身的经验，而不是实际情况。使用这种方法，不可避免地会导致多模态数据无法在公共流形中很好地对齐，最终导致检索性能不佳。为了克服这个 \textbf{CDCP 困境}，我们提出了一种名为 LBUL 的新算法来学习一致的跨模态通用流形（C$^{3}$M），用于基于文本的人物检索。我们方法的核心思想，正如中国俗话说的那样，是“\textit{三思而后兴}”，即\textbf{三思而后行(LBUL)}。 LBUL的通用流形映射机制包括查找步骤和跳跃步骤。与基于CDCP的方法相比，LBUL在将某种模态的数据嵌入到C$^{3}$M之前考虑了视觉和文本模态的分布特征，以实现更可靠的跨模态分布共识，从而实现更高的检索精度。我们在两个基于文本的人物检索数据集 CUHK-PEDES 和 RSTPReid 上评估了我们提出的方法。实验结果表明，所提出的 LBUL 优于以前的方法并实现了最先进的性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为LBUL的新算法，通过学习一致的跨模态公共流形（C³M）来改进基于文本的人检索，解决跨模态分布共识预测（CDCP）方法中的对齐不足问题。
AI.motivation: 现有CDCP方法在映射单模态特征到公共流形时忽视了另一模态的分布特性，导致多模态数据对齐不佳，检索性能次优。
AI.method: LBUL方法包含'看'和'跳'两步：在嵌入数据前，同时考虑视觉和文本模态的分布特征，实现更稳固的跨模态分布共识。
AI.result: 在CUHK-PEDES和RSTPReid数据集上，LBUL方法优于先前方法，达到最先进的检索性能。
AI.conclusion: 通过考虑双模态分布特性，LBUL有效解决了CDCP困境，显著提升了基于文本的人检索效果。

authors_text: Kaizhe Jin,Adrian Rubio-Solis,Ravi Naik,Tochukwu Onyeogulu,Amirul Islam,Salman Khan,Izzeddin Teeti,James Kinross,Daniel R Leff,Fabio Cuzzolin,George Mylonas
title: Identification of Cognitive Workload during Surgical Tasks with Multimodal Deep Learning
title_zh: 利用多模态深度学习识别手术任务期间的认知工作量
abstract: The operating room (OR) is a dynamic and complex environment consisting of a multidisciplinary team working together in a high take environment to provide safe and efficient patient care. Additionally, surgeons are frequently exposed to multiple psycho-organisational stressors that may cause negative repercussions on their immediate technical performance and long-term health. Many factors can therefore contribute to increasing the Cognitive Workload (CWL) such as temporal pressures, unfamiliar anatomy or distractions in the OR. In this paper, a cascade of two machine learning approaches is suggested for the multimodal recognition of CWL in four different surgical task conditions. Firstly, a model based on the concept of transfer learning is used to identify if a surgeon is experiencing any CWL. Secondly, a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) uses this information to identify different degrees of CWL associated to each surgical task. The suggested multimodal approach considers adjacent signals from electroencephalogram (EEG), functional near-infrared spectroscopy (fNIRS) and eye pupil diameter. The concatenation of signals allows complex correlations in terms of time (temporal) and channel location (spatial). Data collection was performed by a Multi-sensing AI Environment for Surgical Task & Role Optimisation platform (MAESTRO) developed at the Hamlyn Centre, Imperial College London. To compare the performance of the proposed methodology, a number of state-of-art machine learning techniques have been implemented. The tests show that the proposed model has a precision of 93%.

Additionally, surgeons are frequently exposed to multiple psycho-organisational stressors that may cause negative repercussions on their immediate technical performance and long-term health. Many factors can therefore contribute to increasing the Cognitive Workload (CWL) such as temporal pressures, unfamiliar anatomy or distractions in the OR. In this paper, a cascade of two machine learning approaches is suggested for the multimodal recognition of CWL in four different surgical task conditions. Firstly, a model based on the concept of transfer learning is used to identify if a surgeon is experiencing any CWL. Secondly, a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) uses this information to identify different degrees of CWL associated to each surgical task. The suggested multimodal approach considers adjacent signals from electroencephalogram (EEG), functional near-infrared spectroscopy (fNIRS) and eye pupil diameter. The concatenation of signals allows complex correlations in terms of time (temporal) and channel location (spatial). Data collection was performed by a Multi-sensing AI Environment for Surgical Task & Role Optimisation platform (MAESTRO) developed at the Hamlyn Centre, Imperial College London. To compare the performance of the proposed methodology, a number of state-of-art machine learning techniques have been implemented. The tests show that the proposed model has a precision of 93%.
abstract_zh: 手术室 (OR) 是一个动态且复杂的环境，由多学科团队组成，在高水平环境中共同努力，提供安全、高效的患者护理。此外，外科医生经常面临多种心理组织压力，这可能会对他们的直接技术表现和长期健康造成负面影响。因此，许多因素可能会增加认知工作量（CWL），例如时间压力、不熟悉的解剖结构或手术室中的干扰。本文提出了两种机器学习方法的级联，用于四种不同手术任务条件下 CWL 的多模态识别。首先，使用基于迁移学习概念的模型来识别外科医生是否正在经历任何 CWL。其次，卷积神经网络 (CNN) 使用此信息来识别与每个手术任务相关的不同程度的 CWL。建议的多模态方法考虑了来自脑电图 (EEG)、功能性近红外光谱 (fNIRS) 和眼睛瞳孔直径的相邻信号。信号的串联允许在时间（时间）和信道位置（空间）方面存在复杂的相关性。数据收集是由伦敦帝国理工学院 Hamlyn 中心开发的用于手术任务和角色优化的多传感人工智能环境平台 (MAESTRO) 进行的。为了比较所提出的方法的性能，已经实施了许多最先进的机器学习技术。测试表明，该模型的准确率达到93%。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种多模态深度学习方法，通过融合EEG、fNIRS和瞳孔直径信号，识别外科手术任务中的认知负荷水平。
AI.motivation: 手术室环境复杂且高压，外科医生面临多种压力源可能导致技术表现下降和健康问题，需有效监测认知负荷以保障手术安全。
AI.method: 采用两阶段机器学习框架：首先基于迁移学习检测认知负荷是否存在，再通过卷积神经网络（CNN）区分不同负荷等级，融合时空特征信号进行分析。
AI.result: 所提模型在四类手术任务中实现93%的精确率，优于现有方法。
AI.conclusion: 多模态深度学习方法能有效识别手术中的认知负荷，为优化手术流程和降低医疗错误提供技术支撑。

search_text: Identification of Cognitive Workload during Surgical Tasks with Multimodal Deep Learning 利用多模态深度学习识别手术任务期间的认知工作量 The operating room (OR) is a dynamic and complex environment consisting of a multidisciplinary team working together in a high take environment to provide safe and efficient patient care. Additionally, surgeons are frequently exposed to multiple psycho-organisational stressors that may cause negative repercussions on their immediate technical performance and long-term health. Many factors can therefore contribute to increasing the Cognitive Workload (CWL) such as temporal pressures, unfamiliar anatomy or distractions in the OR. In this paper, a cascade of two machine learning approaches is suggested for the multimodal recognition of CWL in four different surgical task conditions. Firstly, a model based on the concept of transfer learning is used to identify if a surgeon is experiencing any CWL. Secondly, a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) uses this information to identify different degrees of CWL associated to each surgical task. The suggested multimodal approach considers adjacent signals from electroencephalogram (EEG), functional near-infrared spectroscopy (fNIRS) and eye pupil diameter. The concatenation of signals allows complex correlations in terms of time (temporal) and channel location (spatial). Data collection was performed by a Multi-sensing AI Environment for Surgical Task & Role Optimisation platform (MAESTRO) developed at the Hamlyn Centre, Imperial College London. To compare the performance of the proposed methodology, a number of state-of-art machine learning techniques have been implemented. The tests show that the proposed model has a precision of 93%. 手术室 (OR) 是一个动态且复杂的环境，由多学科团队组成，在高水平环境中共同努力，提供安全、高效的患者护理。此外，外科医生经常面临多种心理组织压力，这可能会对他们的直接技术表现和长期健康造成负面影响。因此，许多因素可能会增加认知工作量（CWL），例如时间压力、不熟悉的解剖结构或手术室中的干扰。本文提出了两种机器学习方法的级联，用于四种不同手术任务条件下 CWL 的多模态识别。首先，使用基于迁移学习概念的模型来识别外科医生是否正在经历任何 CWL。其次，卷积神经网络 (CNN) 使用此信息来识别与每个手术任务相关的不同程度的 CWL。建议的多模态方法考虑了来自脑电图 (EEG)、功能性近红外光谱 (fNIRS) 和眼睛瞳孔直径的相邻信号。信号的串联允许在时间（时间）和信道位置（空间）方面存在复杂的相关性。数据收集是由伦敦帝国理工学院 Hamlyn 中心开发的用于手术任务和角色优化的多传感人工智能环境平台 (MAESTRO) 进行的。为了比较所提出的方法的性能，已经实施了许多最先进的机器学习技术。测试表明，该模型的准确率达到93%。 本文提出一种多模态深度学习方法，通过融合EEG、fNIRS和瞳孔直径信号，识别外科手术任务中的认知负荷水平。 Kaizhe Jin,Adrian Rubio-Solis,Ravi Naik,Tochukwu Onyeogulu,Amirul Islam,Salman Khan,Izzeddin Teeti,James Kinross,Daniel R Leff,Fabio Cuzzolin,George Mylonas cs.LG,cs.AI,cs.HC,eess.SP

authors_text: Valentyn Melnychuk,Dennis Frauen,Stefan Feuerriegel
title: Normalizing Flows for Interventional Density Estimation
title_zh: 标准化介入密度估计的流量
comment: ef:Proceedings of the 40-th International Conference on Machine Learning, Honolulu, Hawaii, USA, PMLR 202, 2023
comments: ef:Proceedings of the 40-th International Conference on Machine Learning, Honolulu, Hawaii, USA, PMLR 202, 2023
abstract: Existing machine learning methods for causal inference usually estimate quantities expressed via the mean of potential outcomes (e.g., average treatment effect). However, such quantities do not capture the full information about the distribution of potential outcomes. In this work, we estimate the density of potential outcomes after interventions from observational data. For this, we propose a novel, fully-parametric deep learning method called Interventional Normalizing Flows. Specifically, we combine two normalizing flows, namely (i) a nuisance flow for estimating nuisance parameters and (ii) a target flow for parametric estimation of the density of potential outcomes. We further develop a tractable optimization objective based on a one-step bias correction for efficient and doubly robust estimation of the target flow parameters. As a result, our Interventional Normalizing Flows offer a properly normalized density estimator. Across various experiments, we demonstrate that our Interventional Normalizing Flows are expressive and highly effective, and scale well with both sample size and high-dimensional confounding. To the best of our knowledge, our Interventional Normalizing Flows are the first proper fully-parametric, deep learning method for density estimation of potential outcomes.
summary: Existing machine learning methods for causal inference usually estimate quantities expressed via the mean of potential outcomes (e.g., average treatment effect). However, such quantities do not capture the full information about the distribution of potential outcomes. In this work, we estimate the density of potential outcomes after interventions from observational data. For this, we propose a novel, fully-parametric deep learning method called Interventional Normalizing Flows. Specifically, we combine two normalizing flows, namely (i) a nuisance flow for estimating nuisance parameters and (ii) a target flow for parametric estimation of the density of potential outcomes. We further develop a tractable optimization objective based on a one-step bias correction for efficient and doubly robust estimation of the target flow parameters. As a result, our Interventional Normalizing Flows offer a properly normalized density estimator. Across various experiments, we demonstrate that our Interventional Normalizing Flows are expressive and highly effective, and scale well with both sample size and high-dimensional confounding. To the best of our knowledge, our Interventional Normalizing Flows are the first proper fully-parametric, deep learning method for density estimation of potential outcomes.
abstract_zh: 现有的因果推理机器学习方法通​​常估计通过潜在结果的平均值（例如平均治疗效果）表达的数量。然而，这些数量并不能捕获有关潜在结果分布的完整信息。在这项工作中，我们根据观察数据估计干预后潜在结果的密度。为此，我们提出了一种新颖的全参数深度学习方法，称为介入归一化流。具体来说，我们结合了两个标准化流程，即（i）用于估计滋扰参数的滋扰流程和（ii）用于潜在结果密度参数估计的目标流程。我们进一步开发了一种基于一步偏差校正的易于处理的优化目标，以有效且双重鲁棒地估计目标流量参数。因此，我们的介入标准化流程提供了正确标准化的密度估计器。通过各种实验，我们证明了我们的介入归一化流具有表现力和高效性，并且可以很好地适应样本大小和高维混杂。据我们所知，我们的介入标准化流程是第一个用于潜在结果密度估计的正确全参数深度学习方法。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于归一化流的干预性密度估计方法，用于从观察数据中估计干预后的潜在结果分布。
AI.motivation: 现有因果推断方法仅估计潜在结果的均值（如平均处理效应），无法捕捉潜在结果的完整分布信息。
AI.method: 设计干预性归一化流方法，结合 nuisance 流（估计混杂参数）和 target 流（参数化潜在结果密度），并基于一步偏差校正开发可 tractable 优化目标。
AI.result: 实验表明该方法在样本量和高维混杂情况下均表现优异，具有高表达能力和估计效率。
AI.conclusion: 干预性归一化流是首个针对潜在结果密度估计的完全参数化深度学习方法，可提供规范化的密度估计。

authors_text: Adyasha Maharana,Darryl Hannan,Mohit Bansal
title: StoryDALL-E: Adapting Pretrained Text-to-Image Transformers for Story Continuation
title_zh: StoryDALL-E：采用预训练的文本到图像转换器来延续故事
comment: ECCV 2022 (33 pages; code, data, demo, model card available at https://github.com/adymaharana/storydalle)
comments: ECCV 2022 (33 pages; code, data, demo, model card available at https://github.com/adymaharana/storydalle)
abstract: Recent advances in text-to-image synthesis have led to large pretrained transformers with excellent capabilities to generate visualizations from a given text. However, these models are ill-suited for specialized tasks like story visualization, which requires an agent to produce a sequence of images given a corresponding sequence of captions, forming a narrative. Moreover, we find that the story visualization task fails to accommodate generalization to unseen plots and characters in new narratives. Hence, we first propose the task of story continuation, where the generated visual story is conditioned on a source image, allowing for better generalization to narratives with new characters. Then, we enhance or 'retro-fit' the pretrained text-to-image synthesis models with task-specific modules for (a) sequential image generation and (b) copying relevant elements from an initial frame. Then, we explore full-model finetuning, as well as prompt-based tuning for parameter-efficient adaptation, of the pre-trained model. We evaluate our approach StoryDALL-E on two existing datasets, PororoSV and FlintstonesSV, and introduce a new dataset DiDeMoSV collected from a video-captioning dataset. We also develop a model StoryGANc based on Generative Adversarial Networks (GAN) for story continuation, and compare it with the StoryDALL-E model to demonstrate the advantages of our approach. We show that our retro-fitting approach outperforms GAN-based models for story continuation and facilitates copying of visual elements from the source image, thereby improving continuity in the generated visual story. Finally, our analysis suggests that pretrained transformers struggle to comprehend narratives containing several characters. Overall, our work demonstrates that pretrained text-to-image synthesis models can be adapted for complex and low-resource tasks like story continuation.
summary: Recent advances in text-to-image synthesis have led to large pretrained transformers with excellent capabilities to generate visualizations from a given text. However, these models are ill-suited for specialized tasks like story visualization, which requires an agent to produce a sequence of images given a corresponding sequence of captions, forming a narrative. Moreover, we find that the story visualization task fails to accommodate generalization to unseen plots and characters in new narratives. Hence, we first propose the task of story continuation, where the generated visual story is conditioned on a source image, allowing for better generalization to narratives with new characters. Then, we enhance or 'retro-fit' the pretrained text-to-image synthesis models with task-specific modules for (a) sequential image generation and (b) copying relevant elements from an initial frame. Then, we explore full-model finetuning, as well as prompt-based tuning for parameter-efficient adaptation, of the pre-trained model. We evaluate our approach StoryDALL-E on two existing datasets, PororoSV and FlintstonesSV, and introduce a new dataset DiDeMoSV collected from a video-captioning dataset. We also develop a model StoryGANc based on Generative Adversarial Networks (GAN) for story continuation, and compare it with the StoryDALL-E model to demonstrate the advantages of our approach. We show that our retro-fitting approach outperforms GAN-based models for story continuation and facilitates copying of visual elements from the source image, thereby improving continuity in the generated visual story. Finally, our analysis suggests that pretrained transformers struggle to comprehend narratives containing several characters. Overall, our work demonstrates that pretrained text-to-image synthesis models can be adapted for complex and low-resource tasks like story continuation.
abstract_zh: 文本到图像合成的最新进展催生了大型预训练转换器，它们具有从给定文本生成可视化效果的出色能力。然而，这些模型不适合故事可视化等专门任务，这需要代理根据相应的标题序列生成一系列图像，从而形成叙述。此外，我们发现故事可视化任务无法适应新叙述中未见过的情节和角色的概括。因此，我们首先提出故事延续的任务，其中生成的视觉故事以源图像为条件，以便更好地概括新角色的叙述。然后，我们使用特定于任务的模块增强或“改造”预训练的文本到图像合成模型，用于（a）顺序图像生成和（b）从初始帧复制相关元素。然后，我们探索预训练模型的全模型微调以及基于提示的调整，以实现参数高效的适应。我们在两个现有数据集 PororoSV 和 FlintstonesSV 上评估我们的方法 StoryDALL-E，并引入从视频字幕数据集中收集的新数据集 DiDeMoSV。我们还开发了一个基于生成对抗网络（GAN）的模型 StoryGANc 用于故事延续，并将其与 StoryDALL-E 模型进行比较，以展示我们方法的优势。我们表明，我们的改造方法在故事延续方面优于基于 GAN 的模型，并且有助于从源图像中复制视觉元素，从而提高生成的视觉故事的连续性。最后，我们的分析表明，经过预先训练的变形金刚很难理解包含多个角色的叙述。总的来说，我们的工作表明，预训练的文本到图像合成模型可以适用于复杂且资源匮乏的任务，例如故事延续。

AI.tldr: 本文提出StoryDALL-E方法，通过改进预训练文本到图像模型，实现基于源图像条件的故事续写任务。
AI.motivation: 现有文本到图像模型难以处理包含新角色和情节的故事可视化任务，缺乏对叙事连续性和泛化能力的支持。
AI.method: 提出故事续写任务，通过添加序列生成模块和图像元素复制模块改进预训练模型，并探索全模型微调和提示调优方法，同时构建新数据集进行评估。
AI.result: 实验表明StoryDALL-E在续写任务中优于GAN模型，能有效复制源图像元素并提升叙事连贯性，但预训练模型仍难以处理多角色叙事。
AI.conclusion: 预训练文本到图像模型可通过任务适配解决复杂叙事任务，但需进一步改进对多角色叙事的理解能力。

We show that our retro-fitting approach outperforms GAN-based models for story continuation and facilitates copying of visual elements from the source image, thereby improving continuity in the generated visual story. Finally, our analysis suggests that pretrained transformers struggle to comprehend narratives containing several characters. Overall, our work demonstrates that pretrained text-to-image synthesis models can be adapted for complex and low-resource tasks like story continuation. 文本到图像合成的最新进展催生了大型预训练转换器，它们具有从给定文本生成可视化效果的出色能力。然而，这些模型不适合故事可视化等专门任务，这需要代理根据相应的标题序列生成一系列图像，从而形成叙述。此外，我们发现故事可视化任务无法适应新叙述中未见过的情节和角色的概括。因此，我们首先提出故事延续的任务，其中生成的视觉故事以源图像为条件，以便更好地概括新角色的叙述。然后，我们使用特定于任务的模块增强或“改造”预训练的文本到图像合成模型，用于（a）顺序图像生成和（b）从初始帧复制相关元素。然后，我们探索预训练模型的全模型微调以及基于提示的调整，以实现参数高效的适应。我们在两个现有数据集 PororoSV 和 FlintstonesSV 上评估我们的方法 StoryDALL-E，并引入从视频字幕数据集中收集的新数据集 DiDeMoSV。我们还开发了一个基于生成对抗网络（GAN）的模型 StoryGANc 用于故事延续，并将其与 StoryDALL-E 模型进行比较，以展示我们方法的优势。我们表明，我们的改造方法在故事延续方面优于基于 GAN 的模型，并且有助于从源图像中复制视觉元素，从而提高生成的视觉故事的连续性。最后，我们的分析表明，经过预先训练的变形金刚很难理解包含多个角色的叙述。总的来说，我们的工作表明，预训练的文本到图像合成模型可以适用于复杂且资源匮乏的任务，例如故事延续。 本文提出StoryDALL-E方法，通过改进预训练文本到图像模型，实现基于源图像条件的故事续写任务。 Adyasha Maharana,Darryl Hannan,Mohit Bansal cs.CV,cs.AI,cs.CL

authors_text: Joseph DiPalma,Lorenzo Torresani,Saeed Hassanpour
title: HistoPerm: A Permutation-Based View Generation Approach for Improving Histopathologic Feature Representation Learning
title_zh: HistoPerm：一种基于排列的视图生成方法，用于改进组织病理学特征表示学习
abstract: Deep learning has been effective for histology image analysis in digital pathology. However, many current deep learning approaches require large, strongly- or weakly-labeled images and regions of interest, which can be time-consuming and resource-intensive to obtain. To address this challenge, we present HistoPerm, a view generation method for representation learning using joint embedding architectures that enhances representation learning for histology images. HistoPerm permutes augmented views of patches extracted from whole-slide histology images to improve classification performance. We evaluated the effectiveness of HistoPerm on two histology image datasets for Celiac disease and Renal Cell Carcinoma, using three widely used joint embedding architecture-based representation learning methods: BYOL, SimCLR, and VICReg. Our results show that HistoPerm consistently improves patch- and slide-level classification performance in terms of accuracy, F1-score, and AUC. Specifically, for patch-level classification accuracy on the Celiac disease dataset, HistoPerm boosts BYOL and VICReg by 8% and SimCLR by 3%. On the Renal Cell Carcinoma dataset, patch-level classification accuracy is increased by 2% for BYOL and VICReg, and by 1% for SimCLR. In addition, on the Celiac disease dataset, models with HistoPerm outperform the fully-supervised baseline model by 6%, 5%, and 2% for BYOL, SimCLR, and VICReg, respectively. For the Renal Cell Carcinoma dataset, HistoPerm lowers the classification accuracy gap for the models up to 10% relative to the fully-supervised baseline. These findings suggest that HistoPerm can be a valuable tool for improving representation learning of histopathology features when access to labeled data is limited and can lead to whole-slide classification results that are comparable to or superior to fully-supervised methods.

Our results show that HistoPerm consistently improves patch- and slide-level classification performance in terms of accuracy, F1-score, and AUC. Specifically, for patch-level classification accuracy on the Celiac disease dataset, HistoPerm boosts BYOL and VICReg by 8% and SimCLR by 3%. On the Renal Cell Carcinoma dataset, patch-level classification accuracy is increased by 2% for BYOL and VICReg, and by 1% for SimCLR. In addition, on the Celiac disease dataset, models with HistoPerm outperform the fully-supervised baseline model by 6%, 5%, and 2% for BYOL, SimCLR, and VICReg, respectively. These findings suggest that HistoPerm can be a valuable tool for improving representation learning of histopathology features when access to labeled data is limited and can lead to whole-slide classification results that are comparable to or superior to fully-supervised methods.
abstract_zh: 深度学习对于数字病理学中的组织学图像分析非常有效。然而，当前的许多深度学习方法需要大量强标记或弱标记的图像和感兴趣区域，而获取这些图像和区域可能非常耗时且资源密集。为了应对这一挑战，我们提出了 HistoPerm，一种使用联合嵌入架构进行表示学习的视图生成方法，可增强组织学图像的表示学习。 HistoPerm 排列从整个幻灯片组织学图像中提取的斑块的增强视图，以提高分类性能。我们使用三种广泛使用的基于联合嵌入架构的表示学习方法：BYOL、SimCLR 和 VICReg，评估了 HistoPerm 在乳糜泻和肾细胞癌的两个组织学图像数据集上的有效性。我们的结果表明，HistoPerm 在准确性、F1 分数和 AUC 方面持续改进了补丁级和幻灯片级分类性能。具体而言，对于腹腔疾病数据集的补丁级分类准确性，HistoPerm 将 BYOL 和 VICReg 提高了 8%，将 SimCLR 提高了 3%。在肾细胞癌数据集上，BYOL 和 VICReg 的补丁级分类精度提高了 2%，SimCLR 提高了 1%。此外，在乳糜泻数据集上，对于 BYOL、SimCLR 和 VICReg，使用 HistoPerm 的模型分别比完全监督的基线模型高 6%、5% 和 2%。对于肾细胞癌数据集，相对于完全监督的基线，HistoPerm 将模型的分类准确率差距降低了 10%。这些发现表明，当标记数据的访问受到限制时，HistoPerm 可以成为改善组织病理学特征的表示学习的宝贵工具，并且可以产生与完全监督方法相当或优于完全监督方法的全玻片分类结果。

AI.tldr: 本文提出HistoPerm方法，通过置换增强视图生成来提升组织病理学图像的特征表示学习。
AI.motivation: 当前深度学习方法依赖大量标注数据，而获取这些数据耗时且资源密集，需开发减少标注依赖的新方法。
AI.method: HistoPerm通过置换全切片图像中提取的补丁的增强视图，结合BYOL、SimCLR和VICReg三种联合嵌入架构进行表示学习。
AI.result: 在两个病理数据集上，HistoPerm显著提升分类性能，如Celiac疾病数据集上BYOL和VICReg的补丁级准确率分别提高8%，SimCLR提高3%；在肾细胞癌数据集上，模型与全监督基线的准确率差距缩小至10%。
AI.conclusion: HistoPerm在标注数据有限时能有效提升病理特征表示学习，其分类结果可与或优于全监督方法。

authors_text: Megh Patel,Devarsh Patel,Sarthak Patel
title: Comparative analysis of segmentation and generative models for fingerprint retrieval task
title_zh: 指纹检索任务分割模型和生成模型的比较分析
comment: This is a working draft and not indented for publication
comments: This is a working draft and not indented for publication
abstract: Biometric Authentication like Fingerprints has become an integral part of the modern technology for authentication and verification of users. It is pervasive in more ways than most of us are aware of. However, these fingerprint images deteriorate in quality if the fingers are dirty, wet, injured or when sensors malfunction. Therefore, extricating the original fingerprint by removing the noise and inpainting it to restructure the image is crucial for its authentication. Hence, this paper proposes a deep learning approach to address these issues using Generative (GAN) and Segmentation models. Qualitative and Quantitative comparison has been done between pix2pixGAN and cycleGAN (generative models) as well as U-net (segmentation model). To train the model, we created our own dataset NFD - Noisy Fingerprint Dataset meticulously with different backgrounds along with scratches in some images to make it more realistic and robust. In our research, the u-net model performed better than the GAN networks
summary: Biometric Authentication like Fingerprints has become an integral part of the modern technology for authentication and verification of users. It is pervasive in more ways than most of us are aware of. However, these fingerprint images deteriorate in quality if the fingers are dirty, wet, injured or when sensors malfunction. Therefore, extricating the original fingerprint by removing the noise and inpainting it to restructure the image is crucial for its authentication. Hence, this paper proposes a deep learning approach to address these issues using Generative (GAN) and Segmentation models. Qualitative and Quantitative comparison has been done between pix2pixGAN and cycleGAN (generative models) as well as U-net (segmentation model). To train the model, we created our own dataset NFD - Noisy Fingerprint Dataset meticulously with different backgrounds along with scratches in some images to make it more realistic and robust. In our research, the u-net model performed better than the GAN networks
abstract_zh: 指纹等生物识别认证已成为现代用户身份验证和验证技术的组成部分。它以比我们大多数人意识到的更多方式普遍存在。然而，如果手指脏、湿、受伤或传感器发生故障，这些指纹图像的质量就会下降。因此，通过去除噪声并修复指纹来重构图像来提取原始指纹对于指纹的认证至关重要。因此，本文提出了一种深度学习方法，使用生成（GAN）和分割模型来解决这些问题。 pix2pixGAN 和 CycleGAN（生成模型）以及 U-net（分割模型）之间进行了定性和定量比较。为了训练模型，我们精心创建了自己的数据集 NFD - 噪声指纹数据集，其中包含不同的背景以及一些图像中的划痕，以使其更加真实和稳健。在我们的研究中，u-net 模型的表现优于 GAN 网络

AI.tldr: 本文通过对比生成模型和分割模型，提出了一种深度学习方法用于解决指纹图像退化问题，以提升指纹认证的准确性。
AI.motivation: 指纹图像可能因手指污损、湿润、受伤或传感器故障而质量下降，导致认证失效，因此需要有效去噪和修复技术。
AI.method: 构建包含不同背景和划痕的噪声指纹数据集（NFD），采用pix2pixGAN、cycleGAN和U-net模型进行训练与对比实验。
AI.result: 实验结果表明，分割模型U-net在指纹修复任务中表现优于生成对抗网络（GAN）模型。
AI.conclusion: 分割模型在指纹图像修复任务中比生成模型更有效，为生物特征认证提供了更可靠的解决方案。

authors_text: Lewis Belcher,Johan Gudmundsson,Michael Green
title: Borch: A Deep Universal Probabilistic Programming Language
title_zh: Borch：一种深度通用概率编程语言
abstract: Ever since the Multilayered Perceptron was first introduced the connectionist community has struggled with the concept of uncertainty and how this could be represented in these types of models. This past decade has seen a lot of effort in trying to join the principled approach of probabilistic modeling with the scalable nature of deep neural networks. While the theoretical benefits of this consolidation are clear, there are also several important practical aspects of these endeavors; namely to force the models we create to represent, learn, and report uncertainty in every prediction that is made. Many of these efforts have been based on extending existing frameworks with additional structures. We present Borch, a scalable deep universal probabilistic programming language, built on top of PyTorch. The code is available for download and use in our repository https://gitlab.com/desupervised/borch.
summary: Ever since the Multilayered Perceptron was first introduced the connectionist community has struggled with the concept of uncertainty and how this could be represented in these types of models. This past decade has seen a lot of effort in trying to join the principled approach of probabilistic modeling with the scalable nature of deep neural networks. While the theoretical benefits of this consolidation are clear, there are also several important practical aspects of these endeavors; namely to force the models we create to represent, learn, and report uncertainty in every prediction that is made. Many of these efforts have been based on extending existing frameworks with additional structures. We present Borch, a scalable deep universal probabilistic programming language, built on top of PyTorch. The code is available for download and use in our repository https://gitlab.com/desupervised/borch.
abstract_zh: 自从多层感知器首次被引入以来，联结主义社区一直在努力解决不确定性的概念以及如何在这些类型的模型中表示不确定性。在过去的十年里，人们在尝试将概率建模的原则方法与深度神经网络的可扩展性结合起来方面付出了很多努力。虽然这种整合的理论上的好处是显而易见的，但这些努力也有几个重要的实际方面；即迫使我们创建的模型能够表示、学习和报告所做的每个预测中的不确定性。其中许多努力都是基于通过额外的结构扩展现有框架。我们推出了 Borch，一种可扩展的深度通用概率编程语言，构建于 PyTorch 之上。该代码可在我们的存储库 https://gitlab.com/desupervised/borch 中下载和使用。

AI.tldr: 本文提出Borch，一种基于PyTorch的深度通用概率编程语言，旨在解决深度学习模型中不确定性表示的问题。
AI.motivation: 传统深度学习模型难以有效处理和表示预测不确定性，现有方法通过扩展框架结构实现概率建模存在局限性。
AI.method: 构建基于PyTorch的通用概率编程语言Borch，提供灵活的不确定性建模框架和可扩展的深度学习整合能力。
AI.result: 实现并开源Borch代码库，为概率深度学习提供统一编程接口，支持不确定性量化与复杂模型构建。
AI.conclusion: Borch为概率深度学习领域提供了新的通用工具，有助于推动不确定性建模在实际应用中的发展。

authors_text: Kuang Gong,Keith A. Johnson,Georges El Fakhri,Quanzheng Li,Tinsu Pan
title: PET image denoising based on denoising diffusion probabilistic models
title_zh: 基于去噪扩散概率模型的PET图像去噪
comment: 8 figures
comments: 8 figures
abstract: Due to various physical degradation factors and limited counts received, PET image quality needs further improvements. The denoising diffusion probabilistic models (DDPM) are distribution learning-based models, which try to transform a normal distribution into a specific data distribution based on iterative refinements. In this work, we proposed and evaluated different DDPM-based methods for PET image denoising. Under the DDPM framework, one way to perform PET image denoising is to provide the PET image and/or the prior image as the network input. Another way is to supply the prior image as the input with the PET image included in the refinement steps, which can fit for scenarios of different noise levels. 120 18F-FDG datasets and 140 18F-MK-6240 datasets were utilized to evaluate the proposed DDPM-based methods. Quantification show that the DDPM-based frameworks with PET information included can generate better results than the nonlocal mean and Unet-based denoising methods. Adding additional MR prior in the model can help achieve better performance and further reduce the uncertainty during image denoising. Solely relying on MR prior while ignoring the PET information can result in large bias. Regional and surface quantification shows that employing MR prior as the network input while embedding PET image as a data-consistency constraint during inference can achieve the best performance. In summary, DDPM-based PET image denoising is a flexible framework, which can efficiently utilize prior information and achieve better performance than the nonlocal mean and Unet-based denoising methods.
summary: Due to various physical degradation factors and limited counts received, PET image quality needs further improvements. The denoising diffusion probabilistic models (DDPM) are distribution learning-based models, which try to transform a normal distribution into a specific data distribution based on iterative refinements. In this work, we proposed and evaluated different DDPM-based methods for PET image denoising. Under the DDPM framework, one way to perform PET image denoising is to provide the PET image and/or the prior image as the network input. Another way is to supply the prior image as the input with the PET image included in the refinement steps, which can fit for scenarios of different noise levels. 120 18F-FDG datasets and 140 18F-MK-6240 datasets were utilized to evaluate the proposed DDPM-based methods. Quantification show that the DDPM-based frameworks with PET information included can generate better results than the nonlocal mean and Unet-based denoising methods. Adding additional MR prior in the model can help achieve better performance and further reduce the uncertainty during image denoising. Solely relying on MR prior while ignoring the PET information can result in large bias. Regional and surface quantification shows that employing MR prior as the network input while embedding PET image as a data-consistency constraint during inference can achieve the best performance. In summary, DDPM-based PET image denoising is a flexible framework, which can efficiently utilize prior information and achieve better performance than the nonlocal mean and Unet-based denoising methods.
abstract_zh: 由于各种物理退化因素和收到的计数有限，PET 图像质量需要进一步改进。去噪扩散概率模型（DDPM）是基于分布学习的模型，它尝试基于迭代细化将正态分布转换为特定的数据分布。在这项工作中，我们提出并评估了不同的基于 DDPM 的 PET 图像去噪方法。在DDPM框架下，执行PET图像去噪的一种方法是提供PET图像和/或先前图像作为网络输入。另一种方法是提供先前图像作为细化步骤中包含的 PET 图像作为输入，这可以适应不同噪声水平的场景。利用 120 个 18F-FDG 数据集和 140 个 18F-MK-6240 数据集来评估所提出的基于 DDPM 的方法。量化表明，包含 PET 信息的基于 DDPM 的框架可以比非局部均值和基于 Unet 的去噪方法产生更好的结果。在模型中添加额外的 MR 先验有助于实现更好的性能并进一步减少图像去噪过程中的不确定性。仅仅依赖 MR 先验而忽略 PET 信息可能会导致较大的偏差。区域和表面量化表明，在推理过程中采用 MR 先验作为网络输入，同时嵌入 PET 图像作为数据一致性约束可以实现最佳性能。综上所述，基于DDPM的PET图像去噪是一种灵活的框架，它可以有效地利用先验信息，并且比非局部均值和基于Unet的去噪方法取得更好的性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出基于去噪扩散概率模型（DDPM）的PET图像去噪方法，通过结合PET图像和先验信息提升去噪效果。
AI.motivation: PET图像因物理退化因素和计数限制存在噪声问题，传统去噪方法（如非局部均值和U-Net）效果有限，需探索更高效的模型框架。
AI.method: 在DDPM框架下设计两种输入策略：1）直接输入PET图像和/或先验图像；2）将先验图像作为输入并在迭代过程中引入PET图像作为数据一致性约束，通过120组18F-FDG和140组18F-MK-6240数据集验证方法有效性。
AI.result: DDPM框架结合PET信息的去噪效果优于非局部均值和U-Net方法，引入MR先验可进一步降低不确定性，但单独依赖MR先验会导致偏差，最佳方案为将MR先验作为输入并嵌入PET数据约束。
AI.conclusion: DDPM为PET图像去噪提供了灵活框架，能有效利用先验信息，在定量和表面分析中均优于传统方法。

authors_text: Md Saiful Islam,Adiba Mahbub,Caleb Wohn,Karen Berger,Serena Uong,Varun Kumar,Katrina Smith Korfmacher,Ehsan Hoque
title: SEER: Sustainable E-commerce with Environmental-impact Rating
title_zh: SEER：具有环境影响评级的可持续电子商务
abstract: With online shopping gaining massive popularity over the past few years, e-commerce platforms can play a significant role in tackling climate change and other environmental problems. In this study, we report that the "attitude-behavior" gap identified by prior sustainable consumption literature also exists in an online setting. We propose SEER, a concept design for online shopping websites to help consumers make more sustainable choices. We introduce explainable environmental impact ratings to increase knowledge, trust, and convenience for consumers willing to purchase eco-friendly products. In our quasi-randomized case-control experiment with 98 subjects across the United States, we found that the case group using SEER demonstrates significantly more eco-friendly consumption behavior than the control group using a traditional e-commerce setting. While there are challenges in generating reliable explanations and environmental ratings for products, if implemented, in the United States alone, SEER has the potential to reduce approximately 2.88 million tonnes of carbon emission every year.
summary: With online shopping gaining massive popularity over the past few years, e-commerce platforms can play a significant role in tackling climate change and other environmental problems. In this study, we report that the "attitude-behavior" gap identified by prior sustainable consumption literature also exists in an online setting. We propose SEER, a concept design for online shopping websites to help consumers make more sustainable choices. We introduce explainable environmental impact ratings to increase knowledge, trust, and convenience for consumers willing to purchase eco-friendly products. In our quasi-randomized case-control experiment with 98 subjects across the United States, we found that the case group using SEER demonstrates significantly more eco-friendly consumption behavior than the control group using a traditional e-commerce setting. While there are challenges in generating reliable explanations and environmental ratings for products, if implemented, in the United States alone, SEER has the potential to reduce approximately 2.88 million tonnes of carbon emission every year.
abstract_zh: 随着过去几年网上购物的广泛普及，电子商务平台可以在应对气候变化和其他环境问题方面发挥重要作用。在这项研究中，我们报告说，先前可持续消费文献所确定的“态度-行为”差距也存在于在线环境中。我们提出SEER，一种在线购物网站的概念设计，帮助消费者做出更可持续的选择。我们引入可解释的环境影响评级，以增加愿意购买环保产品的消费者的知识、信任和便利。在我们对美国 98 名受试者进行的准随机病例对照实验中，我们发现使用 SEER 的病例组比使用传统电子商务环境的对照组表现出明显更环保的消费行为。虽然在为产品提供可靠的解释和环境评级方面存在挑战，但如果实施，SEER 仅在美国就有潜力每年减少约 288 万吨碳排放。

AI.tldr: 本文提出SEER系统，旨在通过环境影响评级帮助消费者在电子商务中做出更可持续的选择。
AI.motivation: 在线购物普及背景下，电子商务平台可通过减少碳排放等措施应对环境问题，但消费者态度与行为存在差距，需有效工具促进可持续消费。
AI.method: 设计SEER系统并开展准随机对照实验，招募98名美国参与者，对比使用SEER与传统电商模式的消费行为差异。
AI.result: 实验显示SEER组消费者表现出更显著的环保行为，若在美国实施，每年可减少约288万吨碳排放。
AI.conclusion: SEER能有效促进可持续消费，但需解决产品环境评级可靠性等挑战以实现广泛应用。

search_text: SEER: Sustainable E-commerce with Environmental-impact Rating SEER：具有环境影响评级的可持续电子商务 With online shopping gaining massive popularity over the past few years, e-commerce platforms can play a significant role in tackling climate change and other environmental problems. In this study, we report that the "attitude-behavior" gap identified by prior sustainable consumption literature also exists in an online setting. We propose SEER, a concept design for online shopping websites to help consumers make more sustainable choices. We introduce explainable environmental impact ratings to increase knowledge, trust, and convenience for consumers willing to purchase eco-friendly products. In our quasi-randomized case-control experiment with 98 subjects across the United States, we found that the case group using SEER demonstrates significantly more eco-friendly consumption behavior than the control group using a traditional e-commerce setting. While there are challenges in generating reliable explanations and environmental ratings for products, if implemented, in the United States alone, SEER has the potential to reduce approximately 2.88 million tonnes of carbon emission every year. 随着过去几年网上购物的广泛普及，电子商务平台可以在应对气候变化和其他环境问题方面发挥重要作用。在这项研究中，我们报告说，先前可持续消费文献所确定的“态度-行为”差距也存在于在线环境中。我们提出SEER，一种在线购物网站的概念设计，帮助消费者做出更可持续的选择。我们引入可解释的环境影响评级，以增加愿意购买环保产品的消费者的知识、信任和便利。在我们对美国 98 名受试者进行的准随机病例对照实验中，我们发现使用 SEER 的病例组比使用传统电子商务环境的对照组表现出明显更环保的消费行为。虽然在为产品提供可靠的解释和环境评级方面存在挑战，但如果实施，SEER 仅在美国就有潜力每年减少约 288 万吨碳排放。 本文提出SEER系统，旨在通过环境影响评级帮助消费者在电子商务中做出更可持续的选择。 Md Saiful Islam,Adiba Mahbub,Caleb Wohn,Karen Berger,Serena Uong,Varun Kumar,Katrina Smith Korfmacher,Ehsan Hoque cs.HC

authors_text: Jun Wang,Samarth Kalluraya,Yiannis Kantaros
title: Verified Compositions of Neural Network Controllers for Temporal Logic Control Objectives
title_zh: 用于时间逻辑控制目标的神经网络控制器的验证组合
abstract: This paper presents a new approach to design verified compositions of Neural Network (NN) controllers for autonomous systems with tasks captured by Linear Temporal Logic (LTL) formulas. Particularly, the LTL formula requires the system to reach and avoid certain regions in a temporal/logical order. We assume that the system is equipped with a finite set of trained NN controllers. Each controller has been trained so that it can drive the system towards a specific region of interest while avoiding others. Our goal is to check if there exists a temporal composition of the trained NN controllers - and if so, to compute it - that will yield composite system behaviors that satisfy a user-specified LTL task for any initial system state belonging to a given set. To address this problem, we propose a new approach that relies on a novel integration of automata theory and recently proposed reachability analysis tools for NN-controlled systems. We note that the proposed method can be applied to other controllers, not necessarily modeled by NNs, by appropriate selection of the reachability analysis tool. We focus on NN controllers due to their lack of robustness. The proposed method is demonstrated on navigation tasks for aerial vehicles.
summary: This paper presents a new approach to design verified compositions of Neural Network (NN) controllers for autonomous systems with tasks captured by Linear Temporal Logic (LTL) formulas. Particularly, the LTL formula requires the system to reach and avoid certain regions in a temporal/logical order. We assume that the system is equipped with a finite set of trained NN controllers. Each controller has been trained so that it can drive the system towards a specific region of interest while avoiding others. Our goal is to check if there exists a temporal composition of the trained NN controllers - and if so, to compute it - that will yield composite system behaviors that satisfy a user-specified LTL task for any initial system state belonging to a given set. To address this problem, we propose a new approach that relies on a novel integration of automata theory and recently proposed reachability analysis tools for NN-controlled systems. We note that the proposed method can be applied to other controllers, not necessarily modeled by NNs, by appropriate selection of the reachability analysis tool. We focus on NN controllers due to their lack of robustness. The proposed method is demonstrated on navigation tasks for aerial vehicles.
abstract_zh: 本文提出了一种新方法，为自主系统设计经过验证的神经网络 (NN) 控制器组合，其任务由线性时序逻辑 (LTL) 公式捕获。特别地，LTL公式要求系统按时间/逻辑顺序到达并避开某些区域。我们假设系统配备了一组有限的经过训练的神经网络控制器。每个控制器都经过训练，可以将系统驱动到特定的感兴趣区域，同时避开其他区域。我们的目标是检查是否存在经过训练的 NN 控制器的时间组合 - 如果存在，则计算它 - 这将产生满足用户指定的 LTL 任务（对于属于给定集合的任何初始系统状态）的复合系统行为。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了一种新方法，该方法依赖于自动机理论和最近提出的神经网络控制系统可达性分析工具的新颖集成。我们注意到，通过适当选择可达性分析工具，所提出的方法可以应用于其他控制器，不一定由神经网络建模。我们关注神经网络控制器，因为它们缺乏鲁棒性。所提出的方法在飞行器导航任务中进行了演示。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种设计满足线性时态逻辑（LTL）任务的神经网络控制器组合方法，并验证其正确性。
AI.motivation: 现有神经网络控制器缺乏鲁棒性，需确保组合后的行为满足时序逻辑要求，这对自主系统导航等关键任务至关重要。
AI.method: 结合自动机理论与神经网络可达性分析工具，验证控制器组合的可行性并生成满足LTL的时序组合。
AI.result: 方法在无人机导航任务中成功验证，证明了其有效性与实用性。
AI.conclusion: 该方法不仅适用于神经网络控制器，还可扩展至其他控制器类型，具有广泛的应用潜力。

authors_text: Karlijn Dinnissen,Christine Bauer
title: A Stakeholder-Centered View on Fairness in Music Recommender Systems
title_zh: 以利益相关者为中心的音乐推荐系统公平性观点
comment: Abstract for FAcctRec 2022 at RecSys 2022, 1 table, main article published in Frontiers in Big Data
comments: Abstract for FAcctRec 2022 at RecSys 2022, 1 table, main article published in Frontiers in Big Data
abstract: Our narrative literature review acknowledges that, although there is an increasing interest in recommender system fairness in general, the music domain has received relatively little attention in this regard. However, addressing fairness of music recommender systems (MRSs) is highly important because the performance of these systems considerably impacts both the users of music streaming platforms and the artists providing music to those platforms. The distinct needs that these stakeholder groups may have, and the different aspects of fairness that therefore should be considered, make for a challenging research field with ample opportunities for improvement. The review first outlines current literature on MRS fairness from the perspective of each stakeholder and the stakeholders combined, and then identifies promising directions for future research. The two open questions arising from the review are as follows: (i) In the MRS field, only limited data is publicly available to conduct fairness research; most datasets either originate from the same source or are proprietary (and, thus, not widely accessible). How can we address this limited data availability? (ii) Overall, the review shows that the large majority of works analyze the current situation of MRS fairness, whereas only few works propose approaches to improve it. How can we move forward to a focus on improving fairness aspects in these recommender systems? At FAccTRec '22, we emphasize the specifics of addressing RS fairness in the music domain.
summary: Our narrative literature review acknowledges that, although there is an increasing interest in recommender system fairness in general, the music domain has received relatively little attention in this regard. However, addressing fairness of music recommender systems (MRSs) is highly important because the performance of these systems considerably impacts both the users of music streaming platforms and the artists providing music to those platforms. The distinct needs that these stakeholder groups may have, and the different aspects of fairness that therefore should be considered, make for a challenging research field with ample opportunities for improvement. The review first outlines current literature on MRS fairness from the perspective of each stakeholder and the stakeholders combined, and then identifies promising directions for future research. The two open questions arising from the review are as follows: (i) In the MRS field, only limited data is publicly available to conduct fairness research; most datasets either originate from the same source or are proprietary (and, thus, not widely accessible). How can we address this limited data availability? (ii) Overall, the review shows that the large majority of works analyze the current situation of MRS fairness, whereas only few works propose approaches to improve it. How can we move forward to a focus on improving fairness aspects in these recommender systems? At FAccTRec '22, we emphasize the specifics of addressing RS fairness in the music domain.
abstract_zh: 我们的叙述性文献综述承认，尽管人们对推荐系统公平性的兴趣总体上越来越大，但音乐领域在这方面受到的关注相对较少。然而，解决音乐推荐系统（MRS）的公平性非常重要，因为这些系统的性能极大地影响音乐流媒体平台的用户和向这些平台提供音乐的艺术家。这些利益相关者群体可能有的独特需求，以及因此应考虑的公平的不同方面，使得研究领域具有挑战性，并有充足的改进机会。该综述首先从每个利益相关者和利益相关者的角度概述了当前关于 MRS 公平性的文献，然后确定了未来研究的有希望的方向。审查中提出的两个悬而未决的问题如下： (i) 在 MRS 领域，仅公开有限的数据来进行公平性研究；大多数数据集要么来自同一来源，要么是专有的（因此无法广泛访问）。我们如何解决数据可用性有限的问题？ (ii) 总体而言，回顾表明，绝大多数著作分析了 MRS 公平性的现状，而只有少数著作提出了改进方法。我们如何才能专注于改善这些推荐系统的公平性？在 FAccTRec '22 上，我们强调了解决音乐领域 RS 公平性的细节。

AI.tldr: 本文通过文献综述探讨音乐推荐系统（MRS）公平性研究的现状与挑战，从利益相关者视角出发，提出未来研究方向。
AI.motivation: 尽管推荐系统公平性研究日益受到关注，但音乐领域的相关研究仍显不足。音乐推荐系统对用户和艺术家均有重大影响，需针对不同利益相关者的公平性需求进行深入研究。
AI.method: 采用叙述性文献综述方法，从用户、艺术家等利益相关者视角分析现有研究，并结合综合视角识别研究空白与未来方向。
AI.result: 发现当前研究多聚焦于现状分析，缺乏改进方法；提出两个关键问题：数据公开性不足及改进公平性的技术路径缺失。
AI.conclusion: 需在音乐推荐系统领域加强数据共享与公平性改进技术研究，以推动更公正的推荐实践。

search_text: A Stakeholder-Centered View on Fairness in Music Recommender Systems 以利益相关者为中心的音乐推荐系统公平性观点 Our narrative literature review acknowledges that, although there is an increasing interest in recommender system fairness in general, the music domain has received relatively little attention in this regard. However, addressing fairness of music recommender systems (MRSs) is highly important because the performance of these systems considerably impacts both the users of music streaming platforms and the artists providing music to those platforms. The distinct needs that these stakeholder groups may have, and the different aspects of fairness that therefore should be considered, make for a challenging research field with ample opportunities for improvement. The review first outlines current literature on MRS fairness from the perspective of each stakeholder and the stakeholders combined, and then identifies promising directions for future research. The two open questions arising from the review are as follows: (i) In the MRS field, only limited data is publicly available to conduct fairness research; most datasets either originate from the same source or are proprietary (and, thus, not widely accessible). How can we address this limited data availability? (ii) Overall, the review shows that the large majority of works analyze the current situation of MRS fairness, whereas only few works propose approaches to improve it. How can we move forward to a focus on improving fairness aspects in these recommender systems? At FAccTRec '22, we emphasize the specifics of addressing RS fairness in the music domain. 我们的叙述性文献综述承认，尽管人们对推荐系统公平性的兴趣总体上越来越大，但音乐领域在这方面受到的关注相对较少。然而，解决音乐推荐系统（MRS）的公平性非常重要，因为这些系统的性能极大地影响音乐流媒体平台的用户和向这些平台提供音乐的艺术家。这些利益相关者群体可能有的独特需求，以及因此应考虑的公平的不同方面，使得研究领域具有挑战性，并有充足的改进机会。该综述首先从每个利益相关者和利益相关者的角度概述了当前关于 MRS 公平性的文献，然后确定了未来研究的有希望的方向。审查中提出的两个悬而未决的问题如下： (i) 在 MRS 领域，仅公开有限的数据来进行公平性研究；大多数数据集要么来自同一来源，要么是专有的（因此无法广泛访问）。我们如何解决数据可用性有限的问题？ (ii) 总体而言，回顾表明，绝大多数著作分析了 MRS 公平性的现状，而只有少数著作提出了改进方法。我们如何才能专注于改善这些推荐系统的公平性？在 FAccTRec '22 上，我们强调了解决音乐领域 RS 公平性的细节。 本文通过文献综述探讨音乐推荐系统（MRS）公平性研究的现状与挑战，从利益相关者视角出发，提出未来研究方向。 Karlijn Dinnissen,Christine Bauer cs.IR,cs.CY,cs.HC

authors_text: Shaochen Wang,Wei Zhang,Zhangli Zhou,Jiaxi Cao,Ziyang Chen,Kang Chen,Bin Li,Zhen Kan
title: What You See is What You Grasp: User-Friendly Grasping Guided by Near-eye-tracking
title_zh: 所见即所得：近眼追踪引导的用户友好型抓取
comment: 6 pages
comments: 6 pages
abstract: This work presents a next-generation human-robot interface that can infer and realize the user's manipulation intention via sight only. Specifically, we develop a system that integrates near-eye-tracking and robotic manipulation to enable user-specified actions (e.g., grasp, pick-and-place, etc), where visual information is merged with human attention to create a mapping for desired robot actions. To enable sight guided manipulation, a head-mounted near-eye-tracking device is developed to track the eyeball movements in real-time, so that the user's visual attention can be identified. To improve the grasping performance, a transformer based grasp model is then developed. Stacked transformer blocks are used to extract hierarchical features where the volumes of channels are expanded at each stage while squeezing the resolution of feature maps. Experimental validation demonstrates that the eye-tracking system yields low gaze estimation error and the grasping system yields promising results on multiple grasping datasets. This work is a proof of concept for gaze interaction-based assistive robot, which holds great promise to help the elder or upper limb disabilities in their daily lives. A demo video is available at https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=yuZ1hukYUrM
summary: This work presents a next-generation human-robot interface that can infer and realize the user's manipulation intention via sight only. Specifically, we develop a system that integrates near-eye-tracking and robotic manipulation to enable user-specified actions (e.g., grasp, pick-and-place, etc), where visual information is merged with human attention to create a mapping for desired robot actions. abstract_zh: 这项工作提出了一种下一代人机界面，可以仅通过视觉推断并实现用户的操作意图。具体来说，我们开发了一个集成近眼跟踪和机器人操作的系统，以实现用户指定的动作（例如抓取、拾取和放置等），其中视觉信息与人类注意力相结合，为所需的机器人动作创建映射。为了实现视觉引导操作，开发了一种头戴式近眼跟踪设备来实时跟踪眼球运动，以便识别用户的视觉注意力。为了提高抓取性能，开发了基于变压器的抓取模型。堆叠式变压器块用于提取分层特征，其中通道的体积在每个阶段扩展，同时压缩特征图的分辨率。实验验证表明，眼睛跟踪系统产生较低的注视估计误差，并且抓取系统在多个抓取数据集上产生有希望的结果。这项工作是基于凝视交互的辅助机器人的概念验证，它有望在日常生活中帮助老年人或上肢残疾者。演示视频位于 https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=yuZ1hukYUrM

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于近眼追踪的用户友好型抓取系统，通过视觉信息和注意力映射实现机器人操作。
AI.motivation: 为老年人及上肢残疾人士提供直观的辅助机器人交互方式，提升日常操作便利性。
AI.method: 开发近眼追踪设备实时捕捉眼球运动，结合基于Transformer的抓取模型，通过分层特征提取优化抓取性能。
AI.result: 眼动追踪系统误差低，抓取模型在多数据集上表现良好，验证了基于注视交互的辅助机器人可行性。
AI.conclusion: 该系统为视线引导的助残机器人提供了概念验证，具有显著的现实应用价值。

authors_text: Neel Guha,Daniel E. Ho,Julian Nyarko,Christopher Ré
title: LegalBench: Prototyping a Collaborative Benchmark for Legal Reasoning
title_zh: LegalBench：构建法律推理的协作基准原型
comment: 13 pages, 7 tables
comments: 13 pages, 7 tables
abstract: Can foundation models be guided to execute tasks involving legal reasoning? We believe that building a benchmark to answer this question will require sustained collaborative efforts between the computer science and legal communities. To that end, this short paper serves three purposes. First, we describe how IRAC-a framework legal scholars use to distinguish different types of legal reasoning-can guide the construction of a Foundation Model oriented benchmark. Second, we present a seed set of 44 tasks built according to this framework. We discuss initial findings, and highlight directions for new tasks. Finally-inspired by the Open Science movement-we make a call for the legal and computer science communities to join our efforts by contributing new tasks. This work is ongoing, and our progress can be tracked here: https://github.com/HazyResearch/legalbench.
summary: Can foundation models be guided to execute tasks involving legal reasoning? We believe that building a benchmark to answer this question will require sustained collaborative efforts between the computer science and legal communities. To that end, this short paper serves three purposes. First, we describe how IRAC-a framework legal scholars use to distinguish different types of legal reasoning-can guide the construction of a Foundation Model oriented benchmark. Second, we present a seed set of 44 tasks built according to this framework. We discuss initial findings, and highlight directions for new tasks. Finally-inspired by the Open Science movement-we make a call for the legal and computer science communities to join our efforts by contributing new tasks. This work is ongoing, and our progress can be tracked here: https://github.com/HazyResearch/legalbench.
abstract_zh: 能否引导基础模型执行涉及法律推理的任务？我们相信，建立一个基准来回答这个问题需要计算机科学和法律界之间的持续合作。为此，这篇简短的论文有三个目的。首先，我们描述 IRAC（法律学者用来区分不同类型的法律推理的框架）如何指导构建面向基础模型的基准。其次，我们提出了根据该框架构建的包含 44 个任务的种子集。我们讨论初步发现，并强调新任务的方向。最后，受到开放科学运动的启发，我们呼吁法律和计算机科学界通过贡献新任务来加入我们的努力。这项工作正在进行中，可以在这里跟踪我们的进展：https://github.com/HazyResearch/legalbench。

AI.tldr: 构建一个协作的法律推理基准测试框架LegalBench，探索基础模型在法律任务中的应用潜力。
AI.motivation: 推动计算机科学与法律领域合作，验证基础模型是否能有效执行法律推理任务，建立标准化评估体系。
AI.method: 基于IRAC法律分析框架设计基准测试，包含44个法律推理任务，并通过开源社区持续扩展任务集。
AI.result: 完成初始44个法律任务的构建，展示基础模型在法律推理中的可行性，提出新任务开发方向。
AI.conclusion: 呼吁跨学科社区共同参与LegalBench建设，通过协作完善法律推理评估体系并推动相关研究进展。
